# CHANGELOG

Machine-friendly drop ledger for SW_MUSH. One entry per delivered
drop. Companion to `TODO.json` (forward-looking) and
`sw_d6_mush_architecture_v51.md` (narrative ground truth).

---

### 2026-06-20 — Building INTERIOR maps wired LIVE: Jedi Temple, Coruscant Works, Gladiator Barracks — *drop interiors-wired-2026-06-20*
Adopts the proven Nano interior pipeline as a live feature (Brian 2026-06-20: "do interiors if it adds to the game"). The 3 building interiors whose room slugs appear in NO existing city map — so binding is collision-free — are wired as real tier-1a AreaGeometries (the `senate_district` pattern, fully data-driven via `AreaGeometryRegistry.load_era`; NO renderer change): each ships `data/worlds/clone_wars/maps/<b>.yaml` (the planet's real interior room slugs + districts + exit-graph corridors + landmark chambers) + a painted `static/maps/<b>_substrate.png` (Opus-picked Nano candidate). On entering one of these rooms the registry binds the slug → the interior area and the client renders the painted floorplan + the live nav overlay (room click-targets, movement paths, labeled landmark pins) — where there was previously NO map. **jedi_temple** (11 rooms, hand-authored zone-map, substrate cand_00), **coruscant_works** (4, industrial sublevel, cand_01), **gladiator_barracks** (6, rock-cut compound, cand_00). The 6 OTHER seedable interiors (Kamino cloning-halls/admin, Geonosis hive/foundry/arena, Mos Eisley cantina) are deliberately NOT wired — their rooms already live in a city map, so a second area claiming the same slugs would make navigation nondeterministic (kept as reviewable drafts under `static/tools/batches/_review/`). Tooling: `tools/make_interior_zonemap.py` projects a planet's interior rooms (`map_x`/`map_y`) into a floorplan zone-map (now emits the loader-required per-room `zone`); `tools/_wire_interiors.py` selects + wires + validates. New `tests/test_interior_maps_wired.py` (4): each loads + carries its substrate + every slug binds to its own area (no collision). Verified: all 3 load+validate, all 21 room slugs resolve to live DB rooms, and 118 map/area/registry/map-safety/smoke tests green. Additive (3 new map YAMLs + 3 PNGs + 1 test); era-clean; no engine/renderer change.

### 2026-06-19 — QA playthrough BLOCKER fixes B1–B6 + force_sensitive sweep — *drop qa-blockers-2026-06-19*
Fixes all 6 confirmed blockers from `docs/design/QA_PLAYTHROUGH_FINDINGS_2026-06-19.md` (the main-session half of Brian's split; the 8 HIGHs go to the Opus loop). **B1** — skill `train` + NPC trainers crashed on a phantom `Character.from_db_row` → `from_db_dict` at `parser/cp_commands.py:157` + `parser/npc_commands.py:120` (swept: the only other `from_db_row` is the real `NPCData.from_db_row`). **B2/B3** — combat `UnboundLocalError: 'wound'` on a stun-KO or a soaked hit on an already-incapacitated target (`engine/combat.py`): `wound` is bound only on the `damage_margin>0` branch but the incapacitation lines referenced `wound.display_name`; now use a new always-bound `incap_word` (the descriptive `wound_text` for damage/stun; the target's CURRENT `wound_level.display_name` when the hit did no damage, so a soaked hit reads "is INCAPACITATED!", not "is NO DAMAGE!"). **B4 + force_sensitive sweep** — `force_sensitive` is DERIVED state (control/sense/alter in the attributes blob); four sites read it off the raw dict/row: `server/session.py:566` HUD (Force PCs lost their Force UI after any logout → now reads the derived `char_obj.force_sensitive`), `parser/force_commands.py::_is_force_being`, `parser/tutorial_commands.py` `training force` gate (H12), `engine/locks.py::LockFlag` — all now derive via `Character.from_db_dict`. (`server/api.py` chargen-submission sites + `game_server.py`'s chain-flag hook verified legitimate, left untouched.) **B5** — housing-lot seeder mapped 37/40 lots to the wrong room + dropped 4 (`engine/housing_lots_provider.py` used the YAML id as `room_id`): new `prime_lots_db_ids(db, era)` resolves each host slug → DB AUTOINCREMENT id via the existing `db.get_room_by_slug` seam (called from `engine/housing.py::seed_lots`), YAML-id fallback for dry-run/test (22 housing tests unchanged). **B6** — `healaccept` drove patient credits negative (`parser/medical_commands.py`): now charges the patient first via `adjust_credits(..., allow_negative=False)` (fresh DB read), aborts with a clear message, and pays the healer ONLY on success (faucet/sink balanced). New `tests/test_qa_blockers_2026-06-19.py` (8 source-guards). Targeted regression: 1,089 tests across the touched modules green. No new top-level systems; funnel functions preserved; era-clean.

### 2026-06-19 — Gate fix: reconcile the Parsec rebrand with the brand/era guards (pre-merge) — *drop parsec-rebrand-test-reconcile*
The full-suite gate before the `drop/sidebar-contract-handoff-capture` → `main` fast-forward surfaced exactly ONE branch-introduced red (from the earlier Parsec rebrand); the other 7 reds are pre-existing `main` baseline (4× onboarding-chain reachability, the laneb fragment-prune, the `loot.md` "storm"≈Stormtrooper era false-positive, the `test_cities_found_feature_flag.py:193` bare-`open()` hygiene offender — none of their files are in the branch diff). Reconciled the rebrand: (a) `tests/test_npc_dialogue_cleanup.py::TestTitleBar` now asserts the live **Parsec** brand in `<title>` (was the stale `"SW MUSH"`; SW_MUSH stays the internal repo codename, "Parsec" is the user-facing brand per `portal.html`), method renamed `test_title_is_parsec_brand`; (b) FIX a stray era bug the rebrand left in the `static/client.html` boot screen — "Star Wars · Galactic Civil War Era" → "Clone Wars Era" (matches `portal.html` + the ~20 BBY invariant; uncaught because the era-scrub test only scans `data/help/`). FLAGGED (pre-existing, not fixed here): `parser/housing_commands.py:547` LLM room-description prompt still says "set during the Galactic Civil War era" — steers generated text to the wrong era; QA/loop backlog.

### 2026-06-19 — Pre-launch QA playthrough campaign: 6 blockers + 8 highs — *drop qa-playthrough-2026-06-19*
Ran the comprehensive QA-playthrough campaign Brian greenlit (multi-agent adversarial playthroughs driving the LIVE game in-process across every subsystem; each blocker/high independently reproduced by a second agent; 9/10 lanes, 25 agents — onboarding_chains died on a session limit, re-run pending). Findings doc: `docs/design/QA_PLAYTHROUGH_FINDINGS_2026-06-19.md`; tracked at `TODO.json` tier_1 `QA.playthrough_2026-06-19`. **6 confirmed BLOCKERS** (all re-verified against HEAD by grep): B1 `train`/NPC-trainer crash (`Character.from_db_row`→`from_db_dict`, parser/cp_commands.py:157 + parser/npc_commands.py:120 — skill progression dead); B2/B3 combat `UnboundLocalError: 'wound'` (engine/combat.py:1853/1872 — soaked-hit-on-incapacitated + stun-KO margin>3); B4 web HUD always `force_sensitive=False` (server/session.py:566 reads the flag off the raw DB dict though it's derived state — Force PCs lose their UI after any logout); B5 housing-lot seeder maps 37/40 lots to the wrong room + drops 4 (engine/housing_lots_provider.py:178 YAML-id vs DB-AUTOINCREMENT, CW era); B6 `healaccept` drives patient credits negative (parser/medical_commands.py:274 stale session-cache affordability + `adjust_credits` default `allow_negative`). Plus 8 HIGHs (chargen cap bypass, combat CP double-spend, buy-order any-resource-string, BountyTrack flat-2D funnel-bypass, mail sidebar permanently dark, `training force` rejects Jedi, raw-`str(e)` leak to players, harness give_item crash). Cross-cutting themes: force_sensitive-raw-dict family (4 sites), stale-session-cache, perform_skill_check funnel bypasses, phantom imports. One claim REFUTED on verify (tutorial 0-exit rooms = working-as-designed). No fixes applied — report only; this is the QA gate that firms the launch estimate.

### 2026-06-19 — Nano map-depth tier test: building INTERIOR works, outer tiers data-blocked — *drop nano-tier-depth-test*
Resolves the `MAP.nano_tier_depth_test` design call (Brian: test painting the tiers the painted-map move dropped, "so I can make the call myself"). A HEAD investigation of all four non-painted tiers (`docs/design/nano_tier_depth_feasibility_2026-06-19.md`) found only the **building INTERIOR** tier has real coordinate data to seed a faithful, non-phantom painting; **planet/system/galaxy** are decorative hardcoded JS fixtures with no backing coordinates (seeding them would paint phantom places — an invariant violation — so the data AFFIRMS the prior D1=B keep-procedural call). PROVEN on the **Jedi Temple**: projected its 11 real `jedi_temple` rooms (`planets/coruscant.yaml` map_x/map_y) into a per-building zone-map (same pattern as `maps/senate_district.yaml`, authored at a test root OUTSIDE `data/worlds/` so it's not wired/phantom), ran the EXISTING `make_substrate_seed.py` (muted recipe) + a nameless interior brief + style anchor — 4 Gemini candidates read as faithful painterly temple interiors (rotunda hub, radiating colonnades, domed council chamber, archives, hangar, garden). No new seed generator needed. Also completed the city tuning: all 8 cities now have good versions (the last 3 — stalgasin/smugglers/tipoca — tuned). ~$2.6 of the $13 refill spent; nothing `select`ed. Map exploration PAUSED here per Brian pending the QA campaign; remaining decision = adopt interior maps as a feature (wire + author more) or leave as proven capability.

### 2026-06-18 — Nano tuning pass: muted-seed recipe (fixes the cross-city artifacts) — *drop nano-styleref-sweep (cont.)*
Follow-up to the $9 sweep. The cross-city generic candidates' worst artifact was the tight-seed's **near-white road centerlines + bright-gold landmark markers** rendering as glowing schematic streaks / token icons. NEW `overnight_runner.py` **`tune`** mode + `regenerate_muted_seed()`: regenerates each city's seed with **muted streets/casing/markers** (`MUTED_STREET/CASING/LM_DIST/LM_GEN`, hue-PRESERVING) + a `_warm_neutral` **de-blue** that neutralises cool district fills Gemini paints as water (skipped for ocean Kamino via `OCEAN_CITIES`), then runs the style-ref batch — worst-first, on its own `_tune` ledger + budget, lock-serialised. Live-validated on kuat_city: the glowing white road-asterisk is gone, roads read as ground-paths (before/after at `static/tools/batches/_review/kuat_before_after.png`). Seed backup/restore is fully non-destructive — a freshly-rendered keymap (most cities ship none) is deleted on restore. Also built per-city contact sheets + a curated `_review/best/` folder (mos_eisley uses the tuned smoke plate; open/desert cities good generic; structured cities use the tuned pass). +4 tests in `tests/test_mapgen_overnight_runner.py` (`_warm_neutral`, muted constants, non-destructive keymap delete, muted seed has no near-white roads). Key ENV-only.

### 2026-06-18 — Nano map-automation: STYLE-REF lever + overnight cross-city sweep — *drop nano-styleref-sweep*
The one untried high-value map-automation lever (HANDOFF §3d) is now PROVEN: feeding the existing hand-made plate as a **style-reference** (2nd image in the Gemini call) pulls output to painterly + away from the tactical-grid/VTT look that sank earlier iterations. A live `mos_eisley` smoke (desert-seed override + nameless brief + style anchor) produced a warm, painterly, water-free, text-free city plate that **rivals the hand-made `mos_eisley_substrate.png`** — flipping the prior "don't switch" verdict to "style-ref is viable; build the comparison library and let Brian adjudicate." (a) NEW `tools/mapgen/style_ref_experiment.py` — reproducible harness (fixes the handoff's "the prior temp script wasn't persisted" gap): backs up → regenerates the warm-desert seed (`WARM_DESERT_HUES`, no slate-blue index-0 that Gemini paints as water; muted markers) → swaps in the nameless dual-image brief → runs the batch with the style anchor → restores in a `finally` (non-destructive). NEW committed `static/tools/seeds/mos_eisley_paint_brief.styleref.md` (nameless + "image1=layout, image2=style" framing). (b) NEW `tools/mapgen/overnight_runner.py` — unattended, **pure-Gemini** (zero Anthropic — survives a Claude compute-limit gap that throttles the Opus/Sonnet loops) cross-city sweep, hard-capped at a configurable Gemini budget (**$9**; Brian raised $5→$9, $10 funded), lock-serialised + ledger-resumable, wrapping each city's brief with a terrain-agnostic style preamble + its own hand-made plate as the anchor; `AREA_PLATE` maps each seed slug to its (non-uniform) plate filename. It NEVER `select`s (never overwrites a real substrate). Scheduled durably via `arm` (reuses `durable_loop`'s hardened task XML). (c) FIX `tools/mapgen/batch.py::select_painting` default slug: a dotted area key now takes its last segment (`tatooine.mos_eisley`→`mos_eisley_substrate.png`), not a `.`→`_` flatten that missed the YAML seam (HANDOFF §3e). New `tests/test_mapgen_style_ref_experiment.py` (6: non-destructive restore, error paths, warm-seed-has-no-water-blue) + `tests/test_mapgen_overnight_runner.py` (6: `AREA_PLATE` covers the sweep + every plate file exists on disk, lock acquire/block, mock-sweep ledger + brief-restore, budget-cap→DONE). Key passed via ENV ONLY (never committed). Cross-city note: generic (stock-seed) style-ref is painterly but shows the seed's markers as artifacts (e.g. senate_district panels/arrows) — per-city tuning (the mos_eisley recipe) is the morning follow-up.
### 2026-06-19 — Species data: B3 era-cleanness fixes — wookiee/bothan/mon_calamari story strings de-OT'd to Clone Wars era (Sonnet loop) — *drop species-b3-era-clean*
Fixed four B3 era violations in `data/species/` that were flagged (but left unfixed) by the Guide_01 quality pass (that drop was engine+data READ-ONLY). All three files had GCW/OT-era production strings visible to players via chargen species selection and `+help species`. **wookiee.yaml**: story_factor "Imperial enslavement of Wookiees is widespread" → "Wookiee warriors stand alongside Republic clone forces in the Clone Wars; Kashyyyk is both strategic ground and sacred home." **bothan.yaml**: description "Many Bothans died to bring the Rebellion the plans to the second Death Star" → "Bothan operatives sell intelligence to Republic and Separatist clients alike when the price is right" (CW-era Bothan spynet flavor; ROTJ reference removed). **mon_calamari.yaml**: description "Rebel Alliance's finest capital ships" → "some of the galaxy's most celebrated capital warships"; story_factor "Mon Calamari are among the Rebel Alliance's strongest supporters" → "Mon Calamari broadly favor the Galactic Republic, valuing its ideals of democracy and justice." New guard `tests/test_species_b3_era_clean.py` (14 tests): per-file surgical assertions + a blanket sweep of all species YAML for B3_TERMS (Imperial/Rebel Alliance/Galactic Empire/TIE Fighter/Death Star). Data-only + test; no engine, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-19 — Guide_18 Jedi Village: authoritative quality pass — Courage 3-way choice + 24h fail lockout, Flesh 6h wall-clock dwell (leavable), Spirit dark-future-self confrontation (4-reject pass / 3-pull Path C / soft-fail reset) (Opus loop) — *drop guide-18-jedi-village*
Opus-owned GUIDES quality pass over **Guide_18 The Jedi Village & Path A/B/C** — the flagship Force-discovery onboarding arc (the only in-Clone-Wars route to playing a Jedi). Cross-checked every Trial/cooldown/path claim vs HEAD (`engine/village_trials.py`, `engine/force_signs.py`, `engine/jedi_gating.py`, `engine/village_choice.py`, `data/worlds/clone_wars/wilderness/dune_sea.yaml`). The guide carried dense **test-invisible** mechanical drift on three of the five Trials:
- **Trial of Courage rewritten.** Guide taught a "multi-turn dialogue" where "easy answers fail / costly answers pass." Reality: Mira delivers a recital, then a single 3-way choice via `trial courage 1|2|3` — `1` ("I won't deny it.") and `2` ("How did you know?") **both pass** (`COURAGE_PASS_CHOICES == (1, 2)`); only `3` (walk away) fails. The test is engage-vs-walk-away, not clever-line selection. **Fail lockout corrected 14-day → 24-hour** (`COURAGE_FAIL_COOLDOWN_SECONDS == 86_400`; "Come back tomorrow"); the 14-day cooldown only gates the gap between *successful* Trials.
- **Trial of Flesh rewritten.** Guide said "you cannot leave; if you leave the room, the Trial fails and you wait the 14-day cooldown." Reality: a **6-hour wall-clock dwell measured from first cave entry** (`FLESH_DURATION_SECONDS == 21_600`, anchored at `village_trial_flesh_started_at`) — Korvas explicitly tells the player *"You may leave the caves; the discipline continues. You may log out; the discipline continues."* No fail-on-leave, no cooldown; it completes automatically when 6h have elapsed. Stated the 6h concretely and dropped the false continuous-presence framing.
- **Trial of Spirit rewritten.** Guide framed it as "Yarael presents a temptation scenario" with a binary refuse/accept. Reality: a confrontation with the player's **dark-future self** (a faction-flavored apparition wearing the PC's older face), up to `SPIRIT_MAX_TURNS == 7` turns, with **three** responses each turn (`trial spirit 1` reject / `2` waver / `3` lean in). Three outcomes, count-driven: **pass clean** at `SPIRIT_REJECTIONS_TO_PASS == 4` rejections; **Path C lock** at `SPIRIT_DARK_PULL_TO_LOCK_C == 3` temptation pulls (still records `village_trial_spirit_done = 1` + `village_trial_spirit_path_c_locked = 1`, irreversible); **soft-fail** if neither threshold is met by turn 7 → trial ends unfinished, **no cooldown**, restart by re-entering. The old binary missed the ambivalent option, the thresholds, and the soft-fail.
- **§8 cooldown table completed** (added the 24h Courage fail-lockout and 6h Flesh-dwell rows, clarified the 14-day applies only between *successful* Trials), **§9 failure-states** de-conflated (per-Trial fail behaviour: Skill 1h / Courage 24h / Flesh none / Spirit soft-fail none — none of them is the 14-day inter-Trial gate), **§10 hidden-info** path-lock line corrected ("lean in three times", not "fail Spirit into Path C"), **§12 command table** fixed (`trial flesh` is a real progress command not "implicit"; added `trial courage`/`trial spirit` + their `1|2|3` rows; `examine fragment_<N>`/`accuse fragment_<N>` precise arg form). Pinned vs HEAD (verified clean, unchanged): Hermit's Hut `(33, 16)` west of the Anchor Stones (== `dune_sea.yaml [33, 16]`), 50h/180,000s playtime gate, 5-sign threshold + 0.0028 base sign probability, Skill `craft_lightsaber` 8/12/15 + 1h retry + kyber-crystal reward, 7-day Act-1→Act-2 + 14-day inter-Trial cooldowns, Path A/B/C rewards/teleports (`jedi_temple_main_gate` + Master Tova Resh / `village_common_square` + Independent +50 rep / `dune_sea_anchor_stones` + `dark_path_unlocked` + `dark_contact_freq`). `tests/test_guide_18_jedi_village.py` (16) pins each correction to its live engine constant so a future retune fails loudly here. Docs/test only, engine+data READ-ONLY, era-clean, no schema/faucet-sink, no avoid-lane (`village_trials.py` dark-future-self prophecy untouched — guide-only re-description per the sanctioned-surface rule).

### 2026-06-19 — Spacer Quest: in-game hint strings fixed (+quest→+spacerquest) + travel command destinations updated (kessel/corellia→kuat/coruscant) (Sonnet loop) — *drop spacer-quest-travel-hints*
Closed the two deferred engine follow-ups flagged by the Guide_25 Spacer Quest quality pass. **(1) In-game `Type '...'` hints corrected:** `engine/spacer_quest.py:1589` quest-start message said `Type '+quest'` — now `'+spacerquest'` (the real command; bare `+quest` routes to the narrative umbrella, not the spacer chain). `parser/spacer_quest_commands.py:83` abandon-confirm prompt said `+quest abandon confirm` — now `+spacerquest abandon confirm`. **(2) TravelCommand destinations updated:** `_DOCKING_NAME_FRAGMENTS` and `_LANDING_NAME_FRAGMENTS` still carried the era-retired `kessel` and `corellia` entries (design v2 replaced them with `kuat`/`coruscant`); those entries have been replaced. Landing fragment for Kuat → `"Kuat - Main Spaceport Arrivals"` (room id 300, `kuat.yaml:44`); docking fragment `"Kuat - Spaceport"` (matches all Kuat spaceport rooms). Coruscant landing → `"Coruscant - Westport Spaceport"` (room id per `coruscant.yaml:55`); docking fragment `"Coruscant - Westport"`. Usage/error strings updated to `"tatooine, narshaddaa, kuat, coruscant"` — matching the Phase-3 Grand Tour `objective_data` (`QUEST_STEPS` step 20). Guard: `tests/test_spacer_quest_travel_hints_fix.py` (12 tests). String/dict edits + test only; no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-19 — Guide_01 Core Mechanics: authoritative quality pass — skill count 75→76, phantom "Clone Trooper Armor" killed, per-system partial-success windows, descending-dice example, CW-era scale examples (Opus loop) — *drop guide-01-core-mechanics*
Opus-owned GUIDES quality pass over **Guide_01 WEG D6 Core Mechanics** — the foundational rules guide every other guide references. Cross-checked every quantified claim vs HEAD (`engine/dice.py`, `engine/skill_checks.py`, `engine/character.py`, `parser/d6_commands.py`, `data/weapons.yaml`, `data/skills.yaml`, `data/species/*`). Fixed four real drifts + one era-polish: (1) **§2 "75 skills"→"76"** — the loaded `SkillRegistry` carries 76 (the `Powersuit Operation` Mechanical skill was added 2026-06-13). (2) **§10 phantom "Clone Trooper Armor" (+1D/+2D/−1D Dex)** — no such registry row; those exact stats are the real `improved_armor` ("Improved Body Armor") → renamed the phantom to the real item. (3) **§4 partial-success** said "(missions, repairs, bounties) … by 4 or fewer" — actually system-specific: repairs `margin >= -4`, missions `margin >= -2`, bounties resolve via combat/capture (no margin window) → rewrote to the accurate per-system thresholds, dropped the bounty claim. (4) **§2 `+roll 4D+2` example** showed normal dice "3, 5, 2" — `roll_d6_pool` sorts them **descending** (`reverse=True`) → fixed to "5, 3, 2". (5) **§6 scale examples** Walker "AT-ST, AT-AT" + Capital "Star Destroyer, Mon Calamari cruiser" were GCW-flavored while the Starfighter row was already CW-era (ARC-170, Vulture Droid) → made Walker "AT-TE, AT-RT" + Capital "Venator-class Star Destroyer, Acclamator-class assault ship". Pinned vs HEAD (all verified clean): §3 Wild Die explode/complication, §4 difficulty ladder (5/10/15/20/25/30) == `Difficulty` enum, §6 scale table (0/2/4/6/9/12/18) == `Scale` enum + char-vs-starfighter diff 6, §7 wound penalties + `can_act` ladder + stun-KO (`len(stun_timers) >= str_dice`) == `WoundLevel`/`Character`, §8 CP/FP mechanics, §10 every weapon/melee/armor damage+cost == `weapons.yaml`, §11 data-flow symbols all importable, §1 Human 18D/2D–4D + Wookiee STR 3D–6D/KNO 2D+1 == species yaml. `tests/test_guide_01_core_mechanics.py` (39). Docs/test only, engine+data READ-ONLY, era-clean, no schema/faucet-sink. **FLAGGED follow-ups (engine, NOT done):** (a) `WoundLevel.WOUNDED_TWICE` (−2D) is defined + rendered on the char sheet ("W.Twice") but the combat damage ladder jumps Wounded→Incapacitated, never producing it — latent reachability gap in `engine/character.py` (avoid-lane). (b) `data/species/wookiee.yaml` story_factor "Imperial enslavement of Wookiees is widespread" is a B3 era violation (Imperial in a production string) — kept this drop guide-only per the engine/data-READ-ONLY pattern.
### 2026-06-19 — Tech debt: v1 TutorialManager dead code removed (Sonnet loop) — *drop tech-debt-tutorial-v1-removal*
`engine/tutorial.py` (the original v1 TutorialManager — a 358-line scripted Mos Eisley guide-droid state machine) was instantiated at `server/game_server.py` but NEVER driven: no call to `.on_enter_game`/`.on_command`/`.skip` existed anywhere in the engine, parser, or server. It was superseded at chargen by the chain-tutorial system (`engine/tutorial_v2` + `engine/tutorial_chains`). Removed `engine/tutorial.py`, the `from engine.tutorial import TutorialManager` import, and the `self.tutorial = TutorialManager()` instantiation. Cleaned up two tests that referenced the now-deleted file: removed the `engine/tutorial.py` hint-check entry from `test_guide_economy_command_syntax.py::_HINT_FILES` (parametrize would have file-not-found) and the stale exclusion comment from `test_ingame_command_hints_resolve.py::_EXCLUDED_FILES`. New regression guard `tests/test_tutorial_v1_removed.py` (3 tests): asserts `engine/tutorial.py` does not exist, `game_server.py` contains no `TutorialManager` reference, and no live source file imports `engine.tutorial` (v1). Closes `TD.TUTORIAL_V1_DEAD_CODE`. Pure deletion; no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-19 — Help corpus: comprehensive misc command gap close-out — quit/semipose/examine/encounter/trade/pay/refine/mail/training/use/restrain/accuse/gate/path + 6 alias fixes (Sonnet loop) — *drop help-misc-command-gaps*
16 new help files + 6 alias-only edits to existing files close ALL remaining player-command help gaps (41 commands with no help entry, reduced to 1: `&`/`@setattr` BUILDER shorthand, intentionally excluded as non-player-facing). New files: `quit.md` (quit/QUIT/logout — save+disconnect), `semipose.md` (;/semipose — name-glued emote), `examine.md` (examine — room/NPC/item detail + Trial of Insight fragment hook), `encounter.md` (encounter/enc/respond/resp/stationact/sa — space encounter status and response), `trade.md` (+trade/trade/offer — player-to-player credit and item exchange with 5% tax), `pay.md` (pay — tribute transfer to pirates), `refine.md` (refine — Onboard Refinery 2:1 raw→refined mid-flight), `mail.md` (+mail/mail — @mail system alias reference), `training.md` (+training/training — Training Grounds facility, distinct from `train` CP advancement), `use.md` (use — activate/consume inventory item, fires on_item_used chain hook), `restrain.md` (cuff/bind/restrain/uncuff/unbind — binder application and release), `allowrestrain.md` (allowrestrain/consentrestrain — voluntary restraint consent), `accuse.md` (accuse — Trial of Insight holocron fragment accusation), `gate.md` (gate — Sister Vitha gate dialogue commit), `path.md` (path — final Force journey path choice, post-trials). Alias additions to existing files: `+faction.md` (+factions/factions), `topics/anomalies.md` (anom), `+place.md` (mu), `board.md` (boardship/boardlink), `+combat.md` ("full dodge"/"full parry"/"combat rolls" multi-word), `+party.md` (pc). Also adds `useforce` alias to `topics/force.md` (the topics entry shadows the command entry; useforce now resolves to the Force overview). New regression guard `tests/test_help_misc_command_gaps.py` (59 tests) verifies each new file, each alias extension, and a full player-command coverage sweep excluding BUILDER-only `&`. Additive markdown + test only; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-19 — Guide_17 Player Shops: authoritative quality pass — phantom `bargain` command killed, buy/sell/order syntax corrected, 10% haggle cap, up-front escrow, relist fee, in-place upgrade (Opus loop) — *drop guide-17-player-shops*
Opus-owned GUIDES quality pass over **Guide_17 Player Shops & Trading** (the passive-revenue commerce guide), cross-checked against `engine/vendor_droids.py`, `engine/skill_checks.resolve_bargain_check`, `parser/shop_commands.py`, the customer `buy`/`sell` routers (`parser/space_commands.py` / `parser/builtin_commands.py`) and `data/vendor_droids.yaml`. The guide carried a cluster of test-INVISIBLE command-surface and mechanical drift (the convention invariant guards the registry, not guide prose):
- **PHANTOM `bargain` command killed.** §1/§4/§5/§13 taught `bargain <slot>` as a customer move. There is no `bargain` command at all — the haggle is applied AUTOMATICALLY inside the customer's `buy`, and only on Tier 2+ droids (`buy_from_droid` runs the Bargain check only `if b_dice > 0 or b_pips > 0`). A **Tier 1 droid never haggles** — it sells at the listed price (the guide previously claimed the opposite: that Tier 1 "customers can always haggle prices down").
- **Haggle cap corrected 10-25% → 10%.** `resolve_bargain_check` maps ±2% per 4 points of opposed-roll margin, capped ±10%. The "10-25% off" prose was wrong.
- **Customer buy syntax** `buy <slot> [quantity]` → **`buy <slot#> from <shop name>`** (or item name); `buy_from_droid` has NO quantity param (one unit per buy).
- **Buy-order fulfilment** `sell <qty> to <shop>` → **`sell <resource> to <shop name>`** (resource name; quantity automatic, up to the order's remaining need).
- **Posting a buy order** `shop order <item> <max_price> [qty]` → **`shop order <resource> <min_quality> <qty> <price_per>`** (four args, crafting-resources only). The old `shop order metal 50 100` example would not even parse.
- **Buy orders are resource-only** (validated vs crafting `RESOURCE_TYPES`) — not "buy completed items from other crafters"; **escrow is deducted up front** at post time (full `qty × price_per`), not gradually as customers sell; the **phantom "max 5 simultaneous orders" cap removed** (no count cap exists — escrow is the limiter). Cancelling refunds unspent escrow.
- **Tier upgrade** is `shop upgrade <tier>` IN PLACE (inventory preserved, price difference charged: gn4→gn7 3,000 / gn7→gn12 7,000 / gn4→gn12 10,000 cr) — §1's "buy a new droid, transfer inventory, sell the old one back for a refund" was phantom (no droid-sellback mechanism exists).
- **Documented the live but previously-omitted recurring relisting fee** (1% of slot value, min 10 cr, on listings idle >30 days, daily `vendor_relist_fee` sink) in §4/§9/§14.
- VERIFIED ACCURATE vs HEAD (now pinned): tier costs/slots/fees/bargain pools (`data/vendor_droids.yaml`), 50% price floor (`PRICE_FLOOR_PCT`), 30/60-day warn/recall (`_WARN_DAYS`/`_RECALL_DAYS`), 2/room + 3/owner droid caps, the four forbidden room types + `no_commerce`, `market search [planet|all]` (bare `market` = cargo prices).
- `tests/test_guide_17_player_shops.py` (32) resolves every taught command against the live registry, asserts `bargain` is NOT a command, and pins every number against the engine constants. Docs/test only; engine + data READ-ONLY; era-clean; no schema, no faucet/sink.
- **FLAGGED follow-up (engine, NOT done — possible balance bug):** the `if b_dice > 0 or b_pips > 0` gate means a **Tier 1 droid never engages the haggle**, so it sells at full sticker — likely the *opposite* of the intended "Tier 1 is easy to haggle down, upgrade to protect prices" design. If intended behavior is that Tier 1 has a weak/zero pool customers easily beat (rather than no haggle at all), the gate should let Tier 1 through `resolve_bargain_check` (which floors the NPC pool at 1D). Guide now documents HEAD's actual behavior; left engine untouched (guides are docs-only). Worth a Brian ruling.
### 2026-06-19 — Help corpus: movement/crime/medical command gap coverage (Sonnet loop) — *drop help-movement-crime-medical-gaps*
7 help files covering movement, roleplay, wilderness navigation, medical consumables, and housing crime: `move.md` (north/south/east/west/ne/nw/se/sw/up/down/enter/leave + abbreviations — all direction aliases resolve to this entry), `think.md` (private IC thoughts — feeds Director AI narrative, invisible to other players), `coords.md` (wilderness grid coordinates, alias `coordinates`), `stim.md` (stim + stimaccept/saccept — all consumable types: stimpack/adrenaline_shot/combat_stim/focus_stim/medpac/medpac_advanced/medpac_fastflesh, self-admin rules, overdose path via `/force` switch), `steal.md` (housing trophy theft — zone security gates: secured=impossible, contested=Sneak+Security 30, lawless=Sneak 15; aliases pilfer/swipe), `forcedoor.md` (force a housing door open, lawless-only, Strength 15; aliases breakin), `escape.md` (break free of binders, Hard Strength 20; alias struggle). New `tests/test_help_movement_crime_medical_gaps.py` (46 tests): file existence, frontmatter key/alias correctness, body content sanity, end-to-end HelpManager.get() resolution for all primary keys and aliases. Additive markdown + test only; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-19 — Help corpus: basic interaction command gap coverage (Sonnet loop) — *drop help-basic-interaction-gaps*
11 player-command help files created covering 30+ command aliases that returned "No help found" via the markdown-loaded HelpManager, plus a game_server.py fix to load rich .md content at boot. New files: `look.md` (look/l — room examination), `equip.md` (equip/wield/draw/wear/don/unequip/holster/sheathe/remove/doff — equipment management umbrella), `buy.md` (buy/purchase — commissary, vendor droid, and cargo purchasing), `sell.md` (sell — NPC vendor, carried items, cargo, vendor droid buy-orders), `give.md` (give/hand — item hand-off to players/NPCs), `loot.md` (loot — looting enemy corpses), `get.md` (get/take/pickup/grab/drop/discard — redirects explaining the no-ground-item model), `train.md` (train — CP-cost skill advancement), `talk.md` (talk/ask — AI-powered NPC dialogue), `board.md` (board/disembark/deboard/leave_ship — ship boarding in bays), `respawn.md` (respawn/revive/bacta — death recovery and bacta treatment). `server/game_server.py` now calls `help_mgr.load_markdown_files()` after `register_topics()` so the rich .md content layers over auto-stubs at runtime (the live `+help` command and the web portal reference page now use the detailed markdown entries). New `tests/test_help_basic_interaction_gaps.py` (73 tests): file existence, frontmatter key/alias correctness, end-to-end HelpManager.get() resolution for all 30+ commands and aliases, body-length sanity. Additive markdown + test + 1-line server fix; no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-19 — Guide_25 Spacer Quest: authoritative quality pass (wrong command +quest→+spacerquest, phase names/boundaries, all reward numbers, Phase-5 geography Corellia→Kuat, phantom debt/loaner mechanics, engine cross-check guard) (Opus loop) — *drop guide-25-spacer-quest*
The Opus-owned guides quality pass over the Spacer Quest ("From Dust to Stars"), the canonical onboarding path for new spacer characters — it had drifted from `engine/spacer_quest.py` in many test-INVISIBLE ways (the curated suite never reads guide prose). **WRONG COMMAND throughout:** the guide taught `+quest` / `+quest log` / `+quest abandon`, but the spacer-quest command is keyed **`+spacerquest`** (aliases `quest` / `+fdts` / `+dusttostars`); plain `+quest` is the *narrative* personal-quest umbrella (`parser.narrative_commands.QuestCommand`), a different system — so a player following the guide hit the wrong command. Rewrote every reference and added an explicit §10 pitfall + §11 disambiguation. **WRONG PHASE NAMES / BOUNDARIES:** Phase 2 is "The Wider Galaxy" (steps **8-14**, seven steps) not "Wider Horizons" (8-13); Phase 3 is "Off-World" (steps **15-20**, six steps) not "Becoming a Spacer" (14-20). "Making Contacts" is the Phase-2 gate (step 14), not a Phase-3 step. Phase names now match the engine's `PHASE_NAMES` exactly (what `+spacerquest` prints). **EVERY REWARD NUMBER corrected** against `QUEST_STEPS`: per-step credits, per-phase totals (P1 2,300 / P2 2,450 / P3 2,000 / P4 2,700 / P5 1,500), and the grand total (**~10,950 cr**, not the guide's ~11,300). Step 20 Grand Tour pays 500 cr + the (Outer Rim Traveler) title (not 1,000 cr); step 25 1,000 cr (not 1,200); step 29 500 cr + (Captain) title (not 300); step 30 1,000 cr + (Spacer) title + 1 CP. **ALL FOUR TITLES documented** ((Outer Rim Traveler) @20, (Versatile Spacer) @25, (Captain) @29, (Spacer) @30) plus the +1 CP grand-completion — and the (Debt Free) title earned on payoff (from `engine/debt.py`) the guide never mentioned. The "Captain" title is awarded at step 29, not as the final reward. **WRONG PHASE-5 GEOGRAPHY:** the 06-01 era migration removed **Kessel and Corellia** from the Clone Wars graph; Lira Shan is a **Kuat Drive Yards broker on Kuat** (re-grounded from CEC/Corellia per `npcs_drop_0a2_fdts_relocate.yaml` + design v2), not a "CEC officer at Coronet Starport on Corellia" — fixed §4/§5/§9 + the step-27 broker. Phase-3 setting corrected to the real four-planet circuit (Tatooine/Nar Shaddaa/Kuat/Coruscant), removing the phantom Kessel/Corellia legs. **PHANTOM MECHANICS removed:** §8 claimed the loaner ship "reverts to its owner at Phase 3 end" — it's the SAME Rusty Mynock from step 15 through the step-27 ownership transfer (`_transfer_ship_ownership`), reclaimed only on abandon; §5/§10 promised missed-debt-payment "patrol scrutiny / lower Hutt rep / collector NPCs come looking" — the real consequence is escalating Grek comlinks at 2 and 3 misses (the enforcer spawn is an unimplemented TODO in `engine/debt.py`). **Start/prereq corrected:** the chain auto-starts on the first post-tutorial NPC conversation (Kessa's comlink, Chalmun's Cantina) once `starter_quest >= 10`, and abandon resets step progress but keeps earned credits/titles/items. Also fixed step 3 (Persuasion vs 10 via Kayson, not "Persuasion or Bargain"), step 4 (Search vs 12, not "Search or Streetwise"), step 5 (complete 3 missions, not "a different type"), step 8 (`+factions`), step 12 (`cpstatus` CP-progress, not `+sheet`), step 24 (talk Renna Dox about crew, not "hire an NPC crew"). New guard `tests/test_guide_25_spacer_quest.py` (17) cross-checks the guide against the live `QUEST_STEPS` / `PHASE_NAMES` data and the engine's reward arithmetic so a future retune that desyncs the guide fails loudly. Docs/content + test only; era-clean; `spacer_quest.py` / `spacer_quest_commands.py` / `debt.py` READ-ONLY (engine untouched). **Flagged for a follow-up fire (engine, not this docs drop):** in-game hints still say `Type '+quest'` (now routes to the narrative umbrella) in `spacer_quest.py:1589` + the abandon confirm string `spacer_quest_commands.py:83`; and the `travel` command still advertises the removed `kessel`/`corellia` destinations (design v2 resolved Kessel→Kuat, Corellia→Coruscant) — both small string fixes for a future spacer-quest-hint drop.

### 2026-06-19 — Help corpus: comms and social command gap coverage (Sonnet loop) — *drop help-comms-gaps*
23 player-facing commands that returned "No help found" now resolve correctly. Fixed by adding aliases to four existing help files and creating one new file. `+channels.md` gains aliases `comlink`/`cl`/`clink` (IC planet comlink), `fcomm`/`fc`/`faction-comm` (faction channel), and `oocsay` (OOC channel alias). `+freqs.md` gains aliases `tune`/`tunein`/`untune`/`tuneout` (frequency subscribe/unsubscribe) and `commfreq`/`cf`/`freq` (transmit on a frequency). `+place.md` gains aliases `tt`/`tabletalk` (table talk), `ttooc` (OOC table talk), and `mutter` (quiet RP emote). `rally.md` gains `+rally` (the `+`-prefixed form). New `data/help/commands/page.md` covers `page`/`p` (private messaging, cross-room tells) with full syntax, re-page usage, and examples. `newbie` was found to already resolve to the built-in New Player Guide key — not a gap. ### 2026-06-19 — Guide_16 Tutorial Chains: authoritative quality pass (phantom +50 rep numbers killed, post-F.8.c.2.e command drift fixed, skip-kit + reward amounts corrected, onboarding guard) (Opus loop) — *drop guide-16-tutorial-chains*
The Opus-owned guides quality pass over the onboarding-critical Tutorial Chains guide — the first thing a new player reads. Verified every command the guide teaches against the SAME live registry `server/game_server` builds and cross-checked every reward/skip/chain-surface claim against `data/worlds/clone_wars/tutorials/chains.yaml`, `skip_starter_kit.yaml`, and the chain engine (`engine/chain_rewards` / `chain_graduation`, `engine/organizations`). **PHANTOM faction-rep numbers removed:** §4/§5/§9 promised "+50" reputation per chain (and a "+30 / +15" split for Smuggler) — but the chains award the literal `graduation.faction_rep` deltas through `adjust_rep`, which total single digits to low teens on the 0–100 scale (Republic Soldier finishes ~9). +50 is the *Honored* tier — a mid-game standing, not a fresh recruit's — so the claim taught new players a wildly inflated starting position. Rewrote all rep claims to "a handful of points (around ten)". **Post-F.8.c.2.e command drift fixed (test-invisible — the convention-invariant guards the registry, not guide prose):** §4 Republic Soldier step 4 taught `board` / `launch`, but tutorial rooms have no walkable exits and the step was re-anchored to `talk` (Pilot CT-7567) by F.8.c.2.e; step 5's NPC is **Sergeant Drix** completing on `+factions`, not a "duty officer" + `comlink`; step 1's "combat sim is east" implied a directional exit that doesn't exist. **Reward amounts / phantom items corrected:** Shipwright graduates with **400** credits (not 500) and grants **no** "schematics for entry-level items" (no such item is awarded); §5 item lists for Republic Intelligence ("lockpick toolkit" → concealed blaster / disguise kit / encrypted comlink) and the other chains re-derived from the data. **Skip starter kit corrected:** §10 said skip-chain alts get "no credits, no items" — they actually receive a small fixed kit (**300 cr + medpac + comlink + hold-out blaster**, no rep/achievement/chain gear) per `skip_starter_kit.yaml`. **Stale surfaces:** `training skip` is now deprecated (points at the chargen skip) — §11/§15 corrected; chargen selects a chain **by number**, not `chain <name>`; the §7 `chain status` example now matches the real `(step N)` + `Completes when: <type>` output (no fabricated "of M"/"Currently in"/"Next" fields). §6 Jedi unlock "five Force-sign landmark visits" verified accurate (`FORCE_SIGNS_FOR_INVITATION == 5`). New guard `tests/test_guide_16_tutorial_chains.py` (35) resolves every taught command, pins the corrected claims, and cross-checks the reward numbers against the live YAML so future data retunes that desync the guide fail loudly. Docs/test only; era-clean; engine + data READ-ONLY.

### 2026-06-19 — Help corpus: space movement + crew station alias coverage (Sonnet loop) — *drop help-space-movement-stations*
QA help-sweep final pass — six ship commands returned "No help found" because they were not covered by any help-file alias. Fixed by adding aliases to existing help files: `data/help/commands/+pilot.md` now covers `launch`/`takeoff`/`land`/`dock` (pilot-only movement verbs) and `copilot`/`copiloting`/`navigator`/`nav` (crew station commands); `data/help/commands/+bridge.md` now covers `engineer`/`eng` (engineer station). `data/help/topics/hyperdrive.md` gains `hyper` (short alias for the already-covered `hyperspace`/`jump`). `+pilot.md` body extended with LAUNCH / LAND, HYPERSPACE, and CREW STATIONS sections so players landing on these entries get real guidance. `hyperspace`/`jump` already resolved to the `hyperdrive` topic — not changed. New `tests/test_help_space_movement_stations.py` (21 tests): alias resolution verified for all 12 new aliases, body-content checks for launch/land/hyperspace/copilot/navigator in +pilot.md and engineer in +bridge.md, regression guard for existing +pilot/+bridge aliases. Additive markdown + test only; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-19 — Guide_08 Force Powers: authoritative quality pass (13-power roster restored, Affect Mind de-dark-sided, DSP tiers + advancement corrected, engine cross-check guard) (Opus loop) — *drop guide-08-force-powers*
The Opus-owned guides quality pass over the Force Powers guide, which had drifted badly from HEAD. Verified every command the guide teaches against the SAME live registry `server/game_server` builds and cross-checked the entire power roster, difficulties, dark-side flags, DSP mechanics and advancement path against the live `engine/force_powers` + `engine/character`. **Roster restored (8 → 13):** the guide documented only the original 8 powers; the Drop 4a / 4a.2 expansion (2026-06-04) added **Telepathy** (Sense, Moderate 15), **Sense Deception** (Sense, Moderate 15), **Farseeing** (Sense, Difficult 20) and **Danger Sense** (Sense, Moderate 15), and split the old mind trick into a light **Affect Mind** + a dark **Dominate Mind** (Difficult 20). §1/§2/§3 rewritten to teach all 13 across Control (3) / Sense (6) / Alter (2) / Combination (2). **CRITICAL correction — Affect Mind is no longer dark side:** the guide marked Affect Mind ⚠️ DARK SIDE / "Always earns 1 Dark Side Point", but `affect_mind.dark_side is False` at HEAD — it is the *light* Jedi mind trick (suggestion); the corrupting counterpart is the new **Dominate Mind** (coercion). The two dark powers are now **Injure/Kill** and **Dominate Mind**. Teaching a player that the canonical Jedi mind trick self-corrupts is a real, safety-relevant error the green suite never caught (the convention-invariant test guards the command registry, not guide prose). Fixed §1, §3 and §5 accordingly. **DSP warning tiers (§5) corrected** to match `_dsp_warning`'s actual bands (1–3 generic point note / 4–5 "the darkness grows within you" / 6+ fall check + "the darkness is consuming you") — the old guide had them off by one tier. Added a brief note on the **Weight of War** fall-check modifier (war-weary Jedi resist the fall less; +2/+5/+10 by tier, extra DSP on a failed resist at the top tier) per `WoW.3c`. **Advancement phantom removed (§7):** the guide claimed Control/Sense/Alter "advance the same way as regular skills" via the `train` command with a 1 / 6 / 15 CP table — but `train` only advances `skills.yaml` skills, and Control/Sense/Alter are not skills there (`train control` is rejected). Rewrote §7 to the real path: Force disciplines are awakened at chargen/Jedi-Village trials and developed through the **Master–Padawan `+teach` bond** (Padawan's CP, guild/bond discount), cross-referencing Guide #14. **Command canonicalization:** §3/§8 now teach `+powers`/`+forcestatus` (A1: OOC/query gets the `+` prefix; bare `powers`/`forcestatus` survive as live aliases). **Telekinesis disarm corrected** from the flavor-string "opposed by their Dexterity" to the real mechanic (beat the difficulty by a margin of 3+, per `DISARM_MARGIN`). New `tests/test_guide_08_force_powers.py` (68): every taught command resolves; the engine roster, each power's difficulty + dark-side flag, the DSP fall threshold, the disarm margin and the not-a-trainable-skill fact are all cross-checked against HEAD so a future engine retune that desyncs the guide fails loudly; the Affect-Mind-is-light and no-`train`-phantom corrections are pinned. Docs/content + test only; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean; `force_powers.py`/`force_commands.py`/`character.py`/`cp_commands.py` read-only (engine untouched).

### 2026-06-19 — Help corpus: +spacerquest/debt/travel — From Dust to Stars commands (Sonnet loop) — *drop help-spacerquest-debt-travel*
Three commands in the "From Dust to Stars" new-player quest chain were missing rich help entries and only had auto-generated stubs. Added `data/help/commands/+spacerquest.md`, `data/help/commands/debt.md`, and `data/help/commands/travel.md`. `+spacerquest` covers the 30-step quest chain (5 phases: Earning Your Keep / The Wider Galaxy / Off-World / A Spacer's Reputation / The Captain's Chair), all three command forms (+spacerquest log / abandon confirm), and the prerequisite (starter chain complete). `debt` covers the Hutt Cartel debt auto-deduction mechanic, the `debt pay <amount>` and `debt payoff` forms, and the overpay guard. `travel` covers the passenger booking flow used only in Phases 2-3, the four valid destinations (tatooine/narshaddaa/kessel/corellia), the docking-bay requirement, and the Phase 4+ redirect (own ship → use launch+hyperspace). New `tests/test_help_spacerquest_debt_travel.py` (21 tests): file-exists, non-empty summary/body/examples for all three, alias resolution (quest→+spacerquest, +fdts, +debt→debt, passage→travel), and see_also cross-links (+spacerquest see_also includes debt and travel). Additive markdown + test only; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane touched.

### 2026-06-19 — Guide_04 Security Zones: authoritative quality pass (phantom +25% lawless CP bonus removed, @security/territory refs corrected, +pvp documented, engine cross-check guard) (Opus loop) — *drop guide-04-security-zones*
The Opus-owned guides quality pass over the Security Zones guide (the EVE-style Secured/Contested/Lawless layer that gates all combat). Verified every command the guide teaches against the SAME live registry `server/game_server` builds and cross-checked every quantified mechanic against the live engine. **Phantom removed:** §6 + §9 promised a **"+25% CP tick-rate bonus while in a lawless zone"** — no such modifier exists. `engine/cp_engine` has exactly four income sources (passive trickle / scene bonus / kudos / AI evaluator) and is entirely zone-blind; standing in a lawless room earns no CP at all. Rewrote both passages to state CP accrues from *play* (missions, scenes, kudos) — which dangerous zones host more of — not from loitering. **Command-reference fixes (test-invisible — the convention-invariant test guards the registry, not guide prose):** §8 mischaracterized `@security override <room> = none` as a generic "clear any override" and omitted the actual `@security override <room> = <faction>` **set form** — the `override` subform is a per-room *faction override* (hostile/unfriendly PCs of the named faction see a SECURED room as LAWLESS), not a security-level toggle. Rewrote §8 to teach all four real subforms and the persistence model (admin `@security` zone-sets write the DB → durable; Director surges/crackdowns are in-memory → reset on restart). §7 still described the **retired per-room** claim model; corrected to the live **per-region** wilderness ownership (`faction claim`; LAWLESS→CONTESTED citadel upgrade for the owning org's members; non-members and independents see base; ceiling is CONTESTED, never SECURED). **Completeness (Opus depth pass):** §2 expanded from "four things" to the full resolution set — added the **room faction-override** (#5) and **player-city citizen upgrade** (#6, CONTESTED→SECURED / LAWLESS→CONTESTED for citizens) factors the engine actually applies in `_finalize`; §3 now documents the **`+pvp` self-flag** (open PvP in CONTESTED only, never overrides SECURED, 5-minute anti-tag-and-flee cooldown) the guide previously omitted; §6 softened the "rare resources ONLY in lawless" overstatement (Contested/Control rooms also carry a T5-rare chance per the harvest yield table). **Mechanics verified accurate against HEAD (now pinned):** §3 challenge TTL = `_PVP_CHALLENGE_TTL` 600s (10 min) + `+pvp` unflag = `PVP_UNFLAG_COOLDOWN_S` 300s (5 min); §2 influence thresholds (underworld ≥80 downgrade / authority ≥75 upgrade / authority ≥90 martial-law forced-SECURED) match `_apply_director_overlay` behaviorally; §5 space-tier mapping (DOCK→secured, ORBIT/HYPERSPACE→contested, DEEP_SPACE→lawless) matches `_SPACE_SECURITY_BY_TYPE`; §7 citadel upgrade matches `_apply_wilderness_ownership`; §4 `+bounty/claim` resolves (umbrella `+bounty`, switch `claim`). New `tests/test_guide_04_security_zones.py` (27): every taught command resolves, the phantom stays dead (no `+25%` in the guide + `cp_engine` asserted zone-blind), and each numeric claim is cross-checked against the engine so a future retune that desyncs the guide fails loudly. Docs/content + test only; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean; `security.py`/`cp_engine.py`/`combat_commands.py`/`harvest.py` read-only (avoid-lane untouched).

### 2026-06-19 — SpaceGrid boot rehydration (LOW verify finding — ships visible after restart) — *drop spacegrid-boot-rehydration*
Fixes the LOW finding from `VERIFY_FINDINGS_2026-06-18.md`: after a server restart, the in-memory `SpaceGrid` was rebuilt empty — undocked, realspace ships were absent from the combat/range/targeting grid until each pilot explicitly relaunched, meaning any session that began shortly after a restart had broken targeting and combat-range for ships that were already in space. Fix: `game_server.start()` now queries `db.get_ships_in_space()` (non-docked rows) immediately before starting the network listeners, filters out ships in hyperspace (correctly absent until `hyperspace_arrival_tick` re-adds them on exit), and calls `self.space_grid.add_ship()` for each realspace ship, mirroring the exact pattern used by `hyperspace_arrival_tick`. Per-ship JSON parse is guarded (`try/except JSONDecodeError`) so one malformed systems blob can't abort the whole rehydration block; the outer try/except makes the whole step fail-open (log warning, game boots). New `tests/test_spacegrid_boot_rehydration.py` (10 tests): basic add-ship/empty-grid, realspace ship added at correct speed, hyperspace ship skipped, mixed fleet, unknown-template fallback speed (5), empty ship list, malformed JSON gracefully treated as empty dict, hyperspace-arrival add-ship non-regression, get_ships_in_space drift guard (SQL must exclude `docked_at IS NULL`). Server/game-server only; no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane. (Sonnet loop)

### 2026-06-19 — Guide_03 Ground Combat: authoritative quality pass (2 broken combat-command references fixed + engine cross-check guard) (Opus loop) — *drop guide-03-ground-combat*
The Opus-owned guides quality pass over the largest, most-played guide (Ground Combat, 38 KB). Verified **every** combat command the guide teaches against the SAME live registry `server/game_server` builds, and cross-checked the §4/§5/§7/§9/§11/§14 mechanics tables against the live engine constants. **Two test-INVISIBLE broken command references fixed** (the convention-invariant test guards the registry, not free-text guide prose, so the unit suite couldn't see these): **(1) §13 posing** taught `> pose <text>` to submit a combat pose — but bare `pose` is the generic room-emote alias (`EmoteCommand` key `emote`, aliases `:`/`pose`/`em`); it broadcasts an ordinary emote and never registers as the round's combat pose, silently leaving the player with the auto-pose. Fixed to the real command **`cpose <text>`** (`CombatPoseCommand`, alias `combatpose`), with an explicit teaching note distinguishing it from the bare `pose`/`:` emote. **(2) §16 quick reference** listed `force-point` (hyphenated) — the registered key is `forcepoint` (aliases `fp`/`+fp`), no hyphenated alias exists, so `force-point` resolved to nothing; fixed to `forcepoint` (or `fp`). Also added the missing `cpose` quick-ref row, aligned the `crolls`/`combat rolls` and `cover [quarter|half|3/4|full]` rows to the engine's actual vocabulary. **Mechanics verified accurate against HEAD (no change needed, now pinned):** §5 damage-margin → wound bands (1–3 Stunned / 4–8 Wounded / 9–12 Incapacitated / 13–15 Mortally Wounded / 16+ Dead) match `WoundLevel.from_damage_margin` at every boundary; §4/§7 cover dice (+1D/+2D/+3D for 1/4·1/2·3/4) match `COVER_QUARTER`/`COVER_HALF`/`COVER_THREE_QUARTER`; §9 aim cap (+3D) matches `min(actor.aim_bonus + 1, 3)`; §11 NPC-AI table lists exactly the five live `CombatBehavior` profiles (aggressive/defensive/cowardly/berserk/sniper); §14 mortal-wound death roll (2D, die if total < rounds mortally wounded) matches `engine/combat.py`. New `tests/test_guide_03_ground_combat.py` (29): every taught command resolves, both fixes pinned (no `force-point`, no bare `> pose` instruction, `cpose` taught), and each mechanics table cross-checked against the engine so a future retune that desyncs the guide fails loudly. Docs/content + test only; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean; combat.py/character.py/npc_combat_ai.py read-only (avoid-lane untouched).

### 2026-06-19 — QA L3 remainder: stale `cp_commands` doubling claim removed + guard test (Sonnet loop) — *drop l3-cp-docstring*
**QA L3 (QA_FINDINGS_2026-06-16.md)** found that `advance_skill()` contained a dead if-branch and a docstring claiming "cost doubled above attribute" — but the code never doubles (WEG R&E rule: cost = current total dice, period). The `advance_skill` fix + tests landed earlier (the source no longer says "doubled", the dead branch is gone). This drop closes the **remaining L3 artifact**: the `parser/cp_commands.py` **module docstring** at line 15 still said `"train costs current_dice CP (doubled if above attribute)"` — wrong, and unchecked by the existing L3 tests (which scope to `Character.advance_skill`). Fixed to `"train costs total_dice CP (WEG R&E rule: current total dice, no doubling)"`. New guard: `TestL3CpCommandsDocstring.test_no_doubled_claim_in_module_docstring` (1 test, added to `tests/test_qa_m4_l3_planet_achievement.py`) — checks the `parser.cp_commands` module docstring for the "doubled" claim so this can't regress silently. Comment-only fix; no engine logic, no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane touched.

### 2026-06-19 — In-game `Type 'X'` command hints: post-rework breakage sweep + durable guard (Opus loop) — *drop ingame-hint-resolve*
The command-syntax rework (Drops 0-7) deleted the bare query/meta forms and made the `+`-prefixed variant canonical. The guide-syntax guards swept player-facing **guide prose**, but the dozens of imperative `Type 'X'` hints emitted from **engine/parser code in live play** were an unguarded surface — a hint that tells a new player to `Type 'missions'` when the bare form was deleted is a "command not found" the unit suite can't see (the convention-invariant test guards the registry, not free-text hint strings). Audited every `Type 'X'` hint in `engine/`/`parser/`/`server/` against the SAME live registry `server/game_server` builds (resolving the base word through aliases + `+cmd/<switch>` bases). Of 30+ hints, the bulk resolve fine (`help`/`?`/`commands` are sanctioned `+help` aliases the rework kept; bare combat verbs `attack`/`dodge`/`aim`/`flee`/`cover`/`range` are registered at their bare keys; `missions`/`bounties`/`powers`/`weapons`/etc. resolve via alias). **Two were genuinely broken and are fixed:** (1) **`engine/spacer_quest.py`** step-8 hint `Type 'factions'` → `Type '+factions'` — bare `factions` was deleted by the rework, so every new spacer following the sector-intel hint hit a dead command. (2) **`parser/space_commands.py`** `@spawn` unknown-template error pointed admins at a nonexistent `Type 'ships'` command → now lists the real available template keys from the in-process registry (`", ".join(sorted(t.key for t in reg.all_templates()))`), non-phantom discovery using the existing `ShipRegistry.all_templates()` API. **Documented non-breakages:** `boarding_link`/`boarding_link_back` are movement EXITS (`engine/boarding.py` `BOARDING_EXIT_DIR_TO`/`_FROM`), resolved by the movement system not the command registry — you cross a boarding link by typing the exit name like `north`; allowlisted. **Tech-debt flagged:** `engine/tutorial.py` (v1 `TutorialManager`) is instantiated at `game_server.py:454` but **never driven** (no `start`/`advance`/`skip` call site anywhere) — superseded by the chain-tutorial system selected at chargen; its `Type 'skip tutorial'` hints are unreachable dead code (the real `training skip` is itself disabled). Excluded from the guard pending removal (`TODO.json` `tech_debt.tutorial_v1_dead_code`). New `tests/test_ingame_command_hints_resolve.py` (4): every live `Type 'X'` hint resolves against the registry (caught **2 additional** would-be false-positive data-type docstrings — `objects` rows `of type 'breachable'` — excluded by a precise regex), allowlist entries are genuinely not commands, + regression pins on both fixes. Chargen-internal hints (`creation.py`/`creation_wizard.py` free-form loop) are excluded — that vocabulary is its own input loop, guarded separately. String/docs + test only; no schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean; no avoid-lane file touched.

### 2026-06-19 — Verify-campaign MEDIUM fixes: rate-bucket address-reuse + IP-throttle empty-key leak — *drop verify-fixes-mem-leaks*
Three memory/correctness fixes from `VERIFY_FINDINGS_2026-06-18.md` MEDIUM findings (Sonnet loop). **(1) Parser rate-bucket address-reuse fix (`parser/commands.py`).** `CommandParser._check_rate_limit` keyed its per-session token bucket on `id(session)` — the CPython memory address, which is reused after GC. A new session created at the same address inherited the previous session's drained bucket, causing cross-session rate-limit bleed (new players briefly unable to type fast). Fixed: key on `session.id` (monotonically increasing `Session._next_id`, never reused). Added `CommandParser.clear_session(session_id)` to remove the stale entry on disconnect. Wired the cleanup into `GameServer` at all three session-removal sites (kicked-existing on token-auth, kicked-existing on password-auth, and normal session teardown). **(2) IP-throttle empty-key leak (`server/api.py`).** `_sliding_window_allow` used `bucket[ip]` (defaultdict — creates the key on first access) and never deleted the key when pruning left it empty. Over time every distinct IP that ever hit a throttled endpoint accumulated a stale empty-list key. Fixed: use `bucket.get(ip, [])` instead (no phantom key creation) and `bucket.pop(ip, None)` when pruning leaves the list empty. This fix covers all three throttle dicts that route through `_sliding_window_allow`: the strict chargen-write limiter (`_rate_limits`), the soft read-endpoint limiter (`_soft_rate_limits`), and the pre-auth connect/create limiter (`_preauth_attempts` in `game_server.py`, which imports this function). **(3) Portal login-throttle empty-key leak (`server/web_portal.py`).** `_login_rate_ok` had the same inline pattern. Fixed to match. New `tests/test_verify_mem_leaks.py` (12 tests): key-delete-on-expiry, no-phantom-key-on-first-get, rate-limit-at-max (sliding window), login-rate cleanup, CommandParser keyed-on-session-id not address, clear_session no-op for unknown id. No schema, no faucet/sink, era-clean, no avoid-lane.

### 2026-06-18 — Help corpus: chain/mastery/lockpick/pickpocket + +bounties alias fix — *drop help-corpus-chain-mastery-thief*
Final help-corpus sweep (Sonnet loop). Added 4 missing command help files and fixed one alias gap: (a) `data/help/commands/chain.md` — tutorial-chain interaction; covers `chain` / `chain status` / `chain attempt` with step-type reference; (b) `data/help/commands/mastery.md` — master-trainer T5 questlines; covers `mastery` / `mastery start` / `mastery status` / `mastery abandon` + unlock workflow; (c) `data/help/commands/lockpick.md` — housing lock-picking; Security skill, difficulty by zone type (Contested 25 / Lawless 20 / Secured impossible), critical-fail jams lock; (d) `data/help/commands/pickpocket.md` — credit theft from sleeping chars; Pickpocket vs Perception−2D, 5–25% credits on success, fumble exposes thief, 10-min cooldown. **Alias fix:** `+bounty.md` aliases included `bounties` but not `+bounties` — `+help +bounties` was returning NOT FOUND while `+help bounties` resolved fine; added `+bounties` to the alias list. All 4 files auto-override the fallback `help_text` entries registered by `HelpManager.auto_register_commands` (markdown wins). New `tests/test_help_chain_mastery_lockpick.py` (22 tests): file-exists, load, frontmatter, HelpManager resolution for all 4 + alias/regression for `pick`→lockpick, `pp`→pickpocket, `+bounties`→+bounty, `masteries`→mastery.

### 2026-06-18 — Concurrency/tick verification fix 4: WS-routing group (area-map memo, combat parallelism, null-room broadcast, dead dispatch) — *drop verify-fix-ws-routing*
Four WS-path findings. (a) **HIGH — area-map blank-until-re-enter:** `send_hud_update`'s `_last_sent_area_key` memo is advanced when the geometry is STAGED into the hud (server/session.py:1420), but the actual send (`:2114`) can fail and `return` — leaving the memo saying "already sent" so the map stays blank until the player leaves + re-enters the area. Fix: on send-failure, if the hud carried `area_geometry`, reset the memo so it re-sends next hud. (b) **HIGH — combat head-of-line blocking:** `_send_combat_state` (parser/combat_commands.py) awaited per-viewer sends in a sequential loop, so one slow/backpressured WS client stalled the whole combat round + the tick loop; now sent via `asyncio.gather(..., return_exceptions=True)`. (c) **MEDIUM — null-room broadcast crash:** `broadcast_chat`'s global branch did `for s in self._sessions` (dict → KEYS/ints) then `s.send_json` → AttributeError; → `.values()` + character guard. (d) **LOW:** removed a dead duplicate `case 'pose_event'` arm in the client WS dispatch (static/client.html). New `tests/test_ws_broadcast_routing.py` (2: global-broadcast iterates values + no duplicate dispatch case); existing combat-state suite green (12).

### 2026-06-18 — Concurrency/tick verification fix 3: tutorial hint-timer leak — *drop verify-fix-hint-timer*
Verification HIGH: `start_hint_timer` (engine/tutorial_v2.py) spawns a detached per-session `_hint_loop` asyncio task but it was only ever cancelled on the player's NEXT command (`on_player_input`) — never at disconnect. A player who enters the tutorial and drops without typing leaves the task looping forever, calling `session.send_line()` on a dead transport every `HINT_REPEAT` (the send error is swallowed, so it never dies); under launch churn these orphan coroutines + `_hint_tasks` entries accumulate. It was also keyed on `id(session)` (a memory address reused after GC → cross-session collision). Fix: rekey `_hint_tasks` on the monotonic `session.id`, and cancel from `SessionManager.remove` (server/session.py) — the single funnel every disconnect path (WS/telnet/web/idle/kick) already routes through (local import avoids a `session`↔`tutorial_v2` cycle). New `tests/test_hint_timer_teardown.py` (2): `remove()` cancels + de-registers the timer; keyed on `session.id` not the address.

### 2026-06-18 — Concurrency/tick verification fix 2: read-pool poisoning — *drop verify-fix-readpool*
Verification HIGH (the B1-read-pool blast-radius risk, reproduced): a `query_only`-rejected write on a pooled read conn left it stuck `in_transaction` (Python's legacy `isolation_level=''` auto-BEGINs before the DML, and SQLite raises *after* the BEGIN with nothing to roll it back). The `_reader` finally returned that conn to the pool as-is, so the held WAL read-transaction **pinned a stale snapshot** (subsequent pool reads served stale data) **and blocked WAL checkpointing** (-wal grew unbounded). Fix (`db/database.py`): open the pool conns with `isolation_level=None` (autocommit → SELECTs never auto-BEGIN, so a rejected write can't open a transaction), plus a self-healing `await conn.rollback()` on release (defense in depth). Corrected the docstrings that overstated "consistent-snapshot." New `tests/test_t321_read_pool.py::test_rejected_write_does_not_poison_pool_conn` (size-1 pool: rejected write → a later committed writer change must be visible, not stale). Suite 10 green.

### 2026-06-18 — Concurrency/tick verification fix 1: the `sm.sessions` blocker — *drop verify-fix-sessions*
The concurrency/tick verification campaign (a Workflow adversarial probe of the never-load-tested runtime surfaces) found a **blocker**: four sites iterate a **nonexistent `<mgr>.sessions`** attribute — the real `SessionManager` exposes `all` / `_sessions` / `count`, never `.sessions`. Effects: `server/tick_handlers_death.py:77` (`getattr(sm,"sessions",[])` → `[]` every tick) made **free time-based wound recovery a permanent no-op** — a player who doesn't pay for bacta stays wounded forever; `engine/debt.py:65` raised AttributeError (swallowed per-debtor) so **Hutt-debt collection collected nothing**; `engine/achievements.py:271` (under a now-false `hasattr(...,"sessions")` guard) and `engine/director.py:1138` were silently degraded. All four → `.all`. The bug hid because the suite's only coverage was `assertTrue(hasattr(tick_handlers_death,"wound_recovery_tick"))` (existence, not behavior) and several test STUBS define a `.sessions` the real manager lacks. New `tests/test_session_manager_accessor_contract.py` (4): a behavioral test proving `wound_recovery_tick` now reaches an enrolled wounded session via `.all` and clears it, + a static guard that no runtime module reintroduces `<mgr>.sessions`. Existing death/recovery suites green (127).

Brian decided the public name (**Parsec**) and said "flip it," so this swaps the working title "Star Wars D6 MUSH" / "SW MUSH" for **Parsec** across every user-visible branding surface — `SW_MUSH`/`sw_mush` stays only as the internal/repo name + db filename. (a) `server/config.py` `game_name` → "Parsec" (propagates to the MSSP `NAME` field + startup logs); the **telnet welcome banner** is rebuilt — the old "STAR WARS" ASCII art → a clean PARSEC block wordmark, and the two movie-quote trademark phrases ("A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away…", "wretched hive of scum and villainy") replaced with original era-clean flavor. (b) `static/portal.html` — `<title>`/og/twitter titles + `og:site_name` + footer + the `<h1>` hero wordmark all → Parsec; the meta descriptions were de-trademarked (kept low-key/SEO per the marketing plan), and the on-page pitch keeps ONE plan-permitted prose reference ("set in the Star Wars galaxy during the Clone Wars"). (c) `static/client.html` — `<title>` + the boot/auth lines → Parsec. New `tests/test_public_name_parsec.py` (4) pins the Parsec branding so it can't silently revert. Guard suite green (era-scrub + MSSP + portal-meta + landing-accuracy, 37 + 4). NOTE: the handoff screenshots (`01_landing_portal`, `02_login_boot`) still show the old name — regenerate at the next capture pass.

### 2026-06-18 — Guides quality pass: comms guide (#21) command accuracy + the `who`→`+who` sweep (Opus loop) — *drop guide-comms-command-syntax*
Opus-owned **guides quality pass** (the Opus loop is the final accuracy gate per guide). `data/guides/Guide_21_Channels_Mail_News.md` is the comms guide every player touches in their first session; it documented bare **`who`** in five places (a `### who` section header, a `who` code block, two quick-reference tables, the §10 numbers row, and the closing "Try `who`" nudge). DROP 1 of the command-syntax rework merged the channel `who` into the builtin `+who` and left **`+who` as the sole key** (no bare `who` key or alias) — so bare `who` now resolves to **nothing**: a "command not found" for any new player following the guide. The convention-invariant test guards the registry, not guide prose, so the suite never caught it. **Verified EVERY command Guide_21 teaches against the SAME live registry `server/game_server` builds** (`say`/`'`/`"`, `tt`, `page`/`p`, `comlink`/`cl`, `fcomm`/`fc`, `commfreq`/`cf`, `ooc`/`newbie`/`oocsay`, `tune`/`untune`, `+freqs`/`freqs`, `+channels`/`channels`, `+news`/`news`, all nine `@mail/<switch>` forms, and the `+scene/start` Scenario-1 cross-ref) — `who` was the **sole** broken command; the rest are accurate. The command-syntax rework (Drops 0-7) deleted the run-on smash keys and bare query forms, but two **player-facing surfaces** still referenced the old vocabulary — invisible to the unit suite (the convention-invariant test guards the registry, not prose / free-text hint strings). ### 2026-06-17 — Sidebar Mail/Achievements contract fix + Claude Design handoff capture-expansion — *drop sidebar-contract-handoff-capture*
**Web producer/consumer bug found while hardening the Claude Design handoff, then fixed.** An audit of the handoff package surfaced that two ground-client sidebar panels were **silently dead on real data**: the M3 `static/client.html` consumers read `data.unlocked` (achievements) and `data.recent`/`m.from` (mail), but the server producers (`server/session.py` `_hud_sidebar_achievements`/`_hud_sidebar_mail` + `parser/achievement_commands.py`) emit `achievements` and `messages`/`from_name` — a key mismatch the M3 rewrite introduced (the legacy `client_legacy.html` reads the canonical keys correctly). Result: the **Achievements panel never rendered** and the **Mail message-list never populated** on live sessions. Fixed by realigning the consumers to the canonical producer shape (`handleAchievementsStatus` → `data.achievements` with a progress readout; `handleMailStatus` → `data.messages`/`from_name`; old keys kept as defensive fallbacks). `handlePlacesStatus` now also surfaces the producer's real `occupants`. New `tests/test_sidebar_panel_contract.py` (7) pins BOTH ends of each contract so a future divergence fails loudly instead of blanking a panel.
**Handoff capture-expansion (Brian: "the game is the scope" / "no corners").** Reworked `tools/capture_ui_screenshots.py` to boot the **real game server** (`main.py`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=""` → paid Director disabled, zero API cost) and capture every surface against it — so chargen's `/api/chargen/*` resolves (the old static `http.server` 404'd it → "Failed to load game data") and the now-fixed sidebar panels render. Set grows **15 → 17**: added **`10_galaxy_navigator`** (the Holocarta wide GALAXY tier — hyperlanes/faction-territory/notable-systems, the major nav surface the prior set entirely missed) and **`03b_chargen_attributes`** (the Allocate-Attributes step). **`04_ground_play`** is now comprehensive — populated **comms pane** (IC/OOC/SYS, the MUSH social core), **faction-standing** + **achievements** + **mail** + **places** sidebar panels, and direction chips (fixed the mock `exits` to the `{dir,label}` object shape the server emits, clearing a `handleHudUpdate` `toUpperCase` throw). `docs/design/CLAUDE_DESIGN_HANDOFF.md` index reordered ascending (01→16) + caveats/gaps/regenerate-language updated. Engine change is the 3 client.html consumers only; rest is docs/tooling.
**Gate fix-forward (run_all_tests.bat):** the full suite surfaced 8 reds; this drop clears the 6 actionable ones. (a) My capture tool's 3 best-effort teardown `except Exception: pass` blocks now log (`taskkill`/`proc.kill`/temp-cleanup) — they were tripping the `test_session38` silent-except invariant (which scans `tools/`). (b) 5 **orphaned command-syntax "zero-behaviour" guards** (`test_command_syntax_drop4`, `test_command_syntax_drop5_stations` ×3, `test_session58_cleanup_umbrellas`) pinned *intermediate* registry states that the rework's terminal DROP 7 deliberately superseded (espionage search re-homed `investigate`→`search`; `order`/`orders` aliases unbound; baseline ratcheted to ZERO). Retargeted each stale assertion to the DROP-7 final state — grounded in CHANGELOG DROP 7 + the authoritative `test_command_syntax_drop7.py` (which passes, proving the current registry is the intended terminal state, not a regression). The remaining 2 reds are pre-existing flaky chain walkthroughs (`republic_soldier`/`republic_intelligence` — both pass solo), unrelated to this drop.

### 2026-06-17 — MSSP telnet option support — *drop marketing-mssp*
Added **MSSP (Mud Server Status Protocol, option 70)** to `server/telnet_handler.py` — the highest-leverage discoverability lever for MUD listing sites (MudVerse, MUDStats, Grapevine auto-index without manual registration). MSSP is sent **proactively on every new telnet connection** via `writer._transport.write()` (raw bytes, bypasses telnetlib3's text encoding which is required for the IAC/SB/SE framing). The payload is `IAC WILL MSSP + IAC SB MSSP <body> IAC SE` where the body is a sequence of `MSSP_VAR <key> MSSP_VAL <value>` byte pairs (UTF-8). Fields: `NAME` (from `config.game_name`), `PLAYERS` (live count via `session_mgr.count`), `UPTIME` (Unix timestamp of handler init), `PORT`, `STATUS=Beta`, `CODEBASE=SW_MUSH`, `LANGUAGE=English`, `CRAWL DELAY=300`. Sent **before** the session is added to `session_mgr` so crawler connections do not inflate the live player count. **Fail-open**: any `Exception` from the transport write is caught+logged at DEBUG, never propagated — MSSP failure cannot abort a real player session. `tests/test_marketing_mssp.py` (15 tests): payload structure guards (IAC WILL header, SB/SE framing), key-value encoding for all 8 required fields, no-IAC-in-body assertion, transport-injection integration (shell writes MSSP bytes on connect), and session-continues-on-transport-failure. No schema change, no faucet/sink, era-clean. Marks `PRELAUNCH.marketing_launch` task 3 (MSSP) DONE.

### 2026-06-17 — T3.19 telemetry breadth: the objective funnel (catalog C) (Opus loop) — *drop t319-objective-telemetry*
**Telemetry BREADTH continuation** of `t319-telemetry-sink` (Brian decision #5: "SINK + BROAD EMITTERS"). The sink + the economy/CP/skill-check chokepoints shipped; this adds the **objective-completion funnel (catalog C)** so post-launch Claude can see whether missions get started-but-abandoned, which tier's pay is too low to bother finishing, etc.
- **NEW `engine.telemetry.emit_objective(kind, phase, char_id, *, oid, reward, **extra)`** — a single `objective` event type carrying `kind` (`mission`/`bounty`/`smuggling`) + `phase` (`start`/`complete`/`abandon`), so the offline funnel is a trivial pivot: `count(start)` vs `count(complete)` vs `count(abandon)` per kind, plus reward distribution per outcome. `char_id` is coerced to `int` when it parses (a str-id and an int-id system join on the same player); `None` extras are dropped; fail-open (wraps the already-fail-open `emit()` + guards field assembly).
- **9 emit sites at the lifecycle chokepoints** of the three board systems (all emitted only AFTER the DB mutation lands, so a record is a real transition): `MissionBoard.accept|complete|abandon` (+`mission_type`,`faction`; abandon captures `accepted_by` **before** it's cleared), `BountyBoard.claim|collect` (+`target`,`alive`; `collect` is the sole completion chokepoint — the auto-collect on kill via `notify_target_killed` routes through it, so one emit covers both paths), `SmugglingBoard.accept|complete|fail|dump_cargo` (+`tier`,`cargo`; `fail` carries `reason`+`fine`, `dump_cargo` `reason="dumped"`). Spacer/communal/chain quests have no single chokepoint (auto-trigger dispatchers) → deferred to a later breadth fire.
- Every call site is double-guarded fail-open: a broken telemetry layer can NEVER break a mission accept / bounty collect / smuggling run. No schema, no faucet/sink (telemetry OBSERVES objective transitions, never creates credits), era-clean. None of the chokepoints live in an avoid-lane file. `tests/test_t319_objective_telemetry.py` (18: helper schema/coercion/None-drop/fail-open + all 9 sites + reject-no-emit + auto-collect-routes-through + a raising-emitter-doesn't-break-accept proof).
