- **Verified:** new `tests/test_director_economy_eyes.py` (8 — totals/net, sorted+capped top lists, empty/zero-movement → `{}`, read-failure fail-open, digest integration with/without db) + director-suite regression green (cw-mapping / adaptive-spend / living-galaxy / bugfixes).
- **Files:** `engine/director.py`, `tests/test_director_economy_eyes.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — T3.20 safe character-load: guard attrs parse + force-sensitivity fail-safe — *drop t3-20-safe-load*
Fixes the two T3.20 launch-blockers (`HANDOFF_t3_20_state_preservation`) — a live save that couldn't load right — both in `engine/character.py::from_db_dict`, read-side, no schema change.
- **Blocker 1 — corrupted attribute locked the player out:** the attributes + force-attribute loops called `DicePool.parse()` **unguarded** (the earlier char-load hardening guarded only the skills loop), so one bad value (e.g. `"4X+2"`) raised and aborted the WHOLE character load — that character could not log in. Now each parse is wrapped (mirrors the skills-loop guard): the bad field is skipped with a warning, the rest of the character loads.
- **Blocker 2 — silent Force downgrade (`FORCE.sensitivity_failsafe_to_jedi`, Brian Ruling 5):** `force_sensitive` is DERIVED from `control`/`sense`/`alter` in the attributes JSON, so a corrupt/pre-derivation blob made a path-committed Jedi silently reconstruct as `force_sensitive=False` (losing their Force on that login). The committed path is recorded in the `village_chosen_path` **typed column** (survives blob corruption), so a committed path (`a`/`b`/`c`) with no recovered Force attrs now **fails SAFE to `force_sensitive=True` + a loud warning** (flagging the Force dice for a backfill) instead of a silent downgrade. `force_sensitive` stays derived state — never persisted (the `save_character` allowlist already blocks it), so this re-asserts safely every load; uncommitted chars get no spurious grant.
- **Deferred follow-ups (noted, not in this drop):** the idempotent Force-attribute *backfill runner* (write-side restore of lost control/sense/alter — `HANDOFF_t3_20_state_preservation`'s one net-new build) and the HANDOFF's HIGH/MEDIUM risk items.
- **Verified:** new `tests/test_t3_20_safe_load.py` (9 — corrupted-attr-loads, corrupted-force-attr, malformed-JSON+committed fail-safe, valid-but-missing-force+committed, path-c, uncommitted-no-grant, missing-column-no-grant, normal-force-unchanged, corrupt-force-value+committed) + 785-test char/village/chargen/powered regression green.
- **Files:** `engine/character.py`, `tests/test_t3_20_safe_load.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Director adaptive-spend governor (slice 1): skip-empty-turns + auto cadence — *drop director-adaptive-spend*
First slice of the Adaptive Spend Governor (`director_scope_and_adaptive_spend_v1.md §5`, Brian decisions D/E). Cadence is the only spend dimension that scales API cost (breadth/depth/memory/Ollama texture are always-on constants), so the `SpendGovernor` is a **cadence controller** that moves `self._turn_interval` within a budget ceiling and skips the paid turn on an empty server. All in `engine/director.py` (no parser collision); the manual `@director fidelity` toggle + the LLM `recommend_fidelity` advisory field are slice 2.
- **Skip-empty-turns (decision D):** `tick()` now routes a due faction turn through `_governed_turn`, which calls `_should_skip_turn` — at ≤1 players online the paid API turn is skipped (an empty server shouldn't pay to narrate to nobody), with a **bounded overnight catch-up**: if the world has been stale past `OVERNIGHT_CATCHUP_SECONDS` (6h) AND players were seen within `OVERNIGHT_CATCHUP_LOOKBACK` (24h), one catch-up turn fires so the world visibly moved by morning. Tracked via `_last_executed_turn_time` (real turns) + `_last_populated_time` (last tick with >1 online).
- **Auto cadence governor (decision E):** `_apply_governor` sets `_turn_interval` for subsequent turns from a cheap ROI heuristic — `FIDELITY_INTERVALS` = eco 60m / standard 30m / high 18m / max 15m. ≥4 players or a high-ROI in-window digest → escalate to `high`; ≤1 → `eco`; else `standard`. **Budget-guarded:** never auto-escalates to `high` once month-to-date spend (`get_budget_stats`) hits the `$30` auto ceiling (`GOVERNOR_AUTO_CEILING_USD`); the `ClaudeProvider` circuit breaker (`monthly_budget_cents`) is the hard backstop underneath. `max` (15m/$40) is reserved for the slice-2 manual toggle.
- **Telemetry:** `governor_state()` snapshot (tier / interval / manual flag / reason) + `_last_fidelity_reason` for the slice-2 `@director status` display; the governor `log`s WHY each cadence change happens.
- **No behavior change when busy:** with ≥2 players the turn runs exactly as before (the governor only sets the next interval). Always-on dimensions untouched.
- **Verified:** new `tests/test_director_adaptive_spend.py` (count-online, skip-empty/overnight-catch-up edge cases, auto tiers, the budget guard, high-ROI digest escalation, manual pin, governor_state, governed-turn skip-vs-run) + 41-test director regression green.
- **Files:** `engine/director.py`, `tests/test_director_adaptive_spend.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Director multi-zone "living galaxy": native CW faction axis + 34-zone load — *drop director-living-galaxy*
Clears `DIRECTOR.zonestate_cw_faction_axis` (Brian's one open design call) and the `DIRECTOR.multizone_living_galaxy` build. Brian ruled **Option A — native CW axis rewrite** (the more faithful path; he delegated the alert-semantics mapping to me under decide+build+log). The Director now tracks **all 34 config zones across every planet + space**, each computing a **correct, distinct** alert from real Clone Wars influence — not the 6 stale Mos Eisley keys that didn't even match the CW config and made every zone read the same wrong alert.
- **The blocker (verified at HEAD):** `VALID_ZONES` was 6 hardcoded keys (`spaceport/streets/cantina/shops/jabba/government`) that don't match the CW config keys (`tatooine_mos_eisley`, `senate_district`, …), so every zone seeded to the 30-fallback; and `ZoneState` hardcoded the GCW axis (`imperial/rebel/criminal/independent`) so `set_faction('republic',80)` silently orphaned and `compute_alert()` read a stale `imperial=50` → **every zone HIGH_ALERT**.
- **ZoneState native rewrite (`engine/director.py`):** influence now lives in a faction-agnostic `scores` dict keyed by the era's `VALID_FACTIONS`. `compute_alert()` reads three alert ROLES (authority/warfront/underworld) resolved from the **existing director_config rewicker** (`imperial→republic`, `rebel→cis`, `criminal→hutt_cartel`) via a new module-level `ALERT_AXIS` — so it's correct for CW **and** the emergency GCW fallback with no new config. `VALID_ZONES = frozenset(DEFAULT_INFLUENCE.keys())` (the 34-zone era config).
- **Alert thresholds (mine, first-cut/playtest-tunable):** authority≥70→LOCKDOWN; underworld≥70→UNDERWORLD; warfront≥40→UNREST; authority<30 & warfront<40 & underworld<40→LAX; authority≥50→HIGH_ALERT; else STANDARD. Preserves the GCW ordering it replaces. `jedi_order` + `bhg` are atmospheric overlays, not primary alert drivers in v1. Result: **12 lockdown / 5 unrest / 5 underworld / 7 standard / 4 lax / 1 high_alert** across the galaxy (verified live).
- **Consumers re-keyed to native CW factions:** digest `zone_influence`; `@director` influence table (both display paths in `director_commands.py`); session HUD `alert_faction` (`server/session.py`); `get_all_zone_states`/debug; the local-nudge heuristic `apply_player_action_deltas` (axis-keyed, era-robust zone selection); **`engine/security.py::_apply_director_overlay`** — the LIVE per-room security overlay now reads native influence (it was **dead for CW**, reading `getattr(zs,"imperial")`=0; senate_district republic=90 → martial law → SECURED, verified). `missions._pick_type` spawn bias now triggers on a **dominant-alert ≥40% share** (neutral at the baseline galaxy — no permanent smuggling skew the old "any zone" check would have introduced with 34 zones).
- **Era cleanness (B3):** removed the GCW production strings in `_generate_local_headline` ("Imperial forces…", "Rebel sympathizers…") and the security-shift notices ("weak Imperial presence") → CW flavor (Republic/Cartel/Separatist/authority).
- **Supersedes** the drop-39 "leave ZoneState axis untouched" contract; `test_director_cw_faction_mapping.py` + `test_b1f_rewicker_boundary.py` updated to the native contract.
- **Noted follow-ups (additive, INERT-not-broken for the 34 zones):** the faction-turn overlay zone map (`_DIRECTOR_ZONE_TO_DB_NAME`/`_BASE_SECURITY`) still covers only the legacy 6 keys (the per-turn overlay is a no-op for CW zones; the live per-room overlay works); smuggling/space `get_alert_level("spaceport")` is inert (was already) — zone-wire later.
- **Verified:** new `tests/test_director_living_galaxy.py` (axis resolution, 34-zone load, scores-dict round-trip/clamp, the original-bug repro, native alert semantics, galaxy differentiation, baseline-neutral spawn bias, era-correct deltas) + updated `test_director_cw_faction_mapping` / `test_b1f_rewicker_boundary` + 228-test director/security/era-cleanness regression green + boot smoke (34 zones load; live overlay reads native influence).
- **Files:** `engine/director.py`, `engine/security.py`, `engine/missions.py`, `parser/director_commands.py`, `server/session.py`, `tests/test_director_living_galaxy.py` (new), `tests/test_director_cw_faction_mapping.py`, `tests/test_b1f_rewicker_boundary.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Director API path works live: SSL fix + 2 verified director.py bugs — *drop 51*
Makes the Director's Claude API path actually reach the API from Brian's box — **verified live end-to-end for the first time** (the real `ClaudeProvider.generate()` returned a completion; previously every live call failed). Three coupled fixes, the prerequisites for the multi-zone "living galaxy" + adaptive-spend build.
- **SSL (the box blocker, root-caused this session):** `ai/claude_provider.py` built a bare `aiohttp.ClientSession()` (certifi default), which fails on Brian's box with `CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED`. Root cause is **Norton Antivirus** "Web/Mail Shield" doing local TLS interception (NOT a corporate proxy — an earlier note misattributed it) — it re-signs certs with a private root in the Windows store that certifi doesn't read. New `_build_ssl_context()` returns a `truststore.SSLContext` (reads the OS store → Norton's root is trusted there → handshake succeeds), injected via a `TCPConnector`. Fail-safe: `None` → aiohttp's certifi default on any non-intercepting box. (Memory `anthropic-api-box-blockers` corrected.)
- **Bug 1 — the dead influence lever (uncaught crash):** `engine/director.py::_run_api_turn` called `self._apply_influence_delta(...)` which was **never defined** → an uncaught `AttributeError` on every API turn that returned `influence_adjustments` (the influence never applied AND the narrative event after it never ran). Now defined: it mutates the in-memory `ZoneState` (`set_faction` clamps to bounds) and persists via `_set_influence`. Correctly targets the Director's OWN `zone_influence` model (string zone keys), NOT the player-territory `adjust_territory_influence` funnel (org_code + int zone_id) — they're distinct influence systems.
- **Bug 2 — cost telemetry logged $0:** `_run_api_turn` hardcoded `tok_in=tok_out=0`, so `director_log` always recorded zero cost and `estimated_cost_usd` always reported $0 — which the cost circuit-breaker and the upcoming adaptive-spend ceiling depend on. The provider now captures each call's usage (`self._last_usage`) and exposes `last_usage()`; the Director reads it to log REAL token counts. (Confirmed live: 20 in / 7 out on the smoke call.)
- **Verified:** `tests/test_director_bugfixes.py` (9 — the lever defined/async/applies-delta/clamps/zero-noop/unknown-zone-safe, `last_usage` default + copy-safety, the SSL context builds) + a LIVE end-to-end `generate()` smoke (real completion + real usage) + 208-test director/provider/territory/influence regression green.
- **Files:** `ai/claude_provider.py`, `engine/director.py`, `tests/test_director_bugfixes.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Lore-ingestion toolchain + full sourcebook ingestion (lorebook 74→1,978 entries) — *tooling*
Sourcebook → lorebook pipeline so the WEG D6 corpus can ground both AI layers (Director + NPC dialogue). All tools live in `tools/`, are review-file-only (never edit the live lorebook directly), and share one `_BANNED` era-guard + canonical-figure check. Pairs with `sourcebook_ingestion_pipeline_v1.md` + `sourcebook_grounding_reprioritization_v1.md`.
- **`tools/ingest_lore.py`** — PDF → OCR (PyMuPDF text layer; Tesseract fallback on scanned pages, auto-detected at the Windows install path) → Haiku era-translation to ~20 BBY → era-guard → review yaml with `planet`/`surface_affinity` filter tags. **Hardened this session:** `truststore` injection (the dev box sits behind a Lockheed TLS-inspection proxy that breaks Python's default SSL — certifi can't fix it, the Windows cert store can) + a loud fatal-abort so an auth/credit/SSL failure can't masquerade as a silent 0-entry run (verified: exit 3 with the cause + fix, no file written) + UTF-8 console safety.
- **`tools/harvest_extractions.py`** — $0 harvest of the "Deliverable A" lore dicts already sitting in the 16 `docs/design/*extraction*.md` docs, through the same era-guard. 45 clean entries (13 correctly rejected as genuine GCW leaks, e.g. "General Airen Cracken").
- **`tools/append_lore.py`** (new) — safe additive appender: parses a review yaml's keepers, dedups by title against the live file + within the batch, **comment-preserving text append** (no YAML round-trip, per the map-safety invariant), backs up to `.bak`, then **re-validates the whole file against the loader's hard rules and rolls back on any failure** (a malformed append can never block seeding). Era-rechecks every keeper.
- **`tools/ingest_batch.py`** (new) — one-command runner for the grounding-priority book set (under-grounded planets first: Coruscant/Kuat/Nar Shaddaa), sequential (no OCR thrash), stops on a fatal API error instead of hammering.
- **Step 1 — free harvest:** 33 harvested entries appended (45 minus 12 already-present) → **74 → 107**.
- **Concurrency + robustness (added when the paid run scaled up):** opt-in `--concurrency` runs OCR pages AND API chunks in parallel — thread-safe (pixmaps rendered sequentially since PyMuPDF isn't thread-safe on one Document, then Tesseract run concurrently on the PNG bytes) — with 429 retry-with-backoff + read-timeout retry so parallel load never silently drops a chunk. OCR bumped 200→300 DPI; the extract prompt now tells the model to silently fix obvious OCR artifacts and **omit (not invent)** anything too garbled. Cut Coruscant (162 fully-scanned pages) from a 20-min sequential stall to a clean run.
- **Step 2 — full 12-book ingestion (after the account was funded):** 12 WEG/d20 sourcebooks → **2,023 clean** extracted entries. An LLM **quality-gate** (12-reviewer workflow) then caught **61 subtle drops the static `_BANNED` list can't see** — notably **"New Republic"/"Grand Moff"/"Mon Mothma" era leaks** (those tokens aren't in `_BANNED`), post-war "civil war" phrasing, and OCR-garbled proper nouns ("Grand Moll Bank"). Curated append (exclude the 61, `priority ≥5` floor −81, dedup −10) → **107 → 1,978 entries**, parses clean, lore-loader + era-cleanness tests green. Coverage: Coruscant 111 / Kuat 35 (the two worst gaps, filled) + 1,695 era-neutral "all". Inert until the next server reseed.
- **Follow-up surfaced:** `_BANNED` should gain the post-CW/New-Republic tokens (New Republic, Grand Moff/Moff, Mon Mothma…) so the runtime era-guard + static tests catch what only the LLM gate caught here. (The Director's latent SSL bug this entry originally flagged was independently fixed by drop 51 — `truststore` in `claude_provider.py`.)
- **Decisions recorded:** keep keyword+tag lorebook retrieval (no vector RAG; FTS5 is the next rung if ever needed); quest/scenario expansion ruled POST-LAUNCH-OK with the painless path verified at code level (`quest_expansion_postlaunch_path_v1.md`, TODO T3.24).
- **Files:** `tools/ingest_lore.py`, `tools/harvest_extractions.py`, `tools/append_lore.py` (new), `tools/ingest_batch.py` (new), `data/worlds/clone_wars/lore.yaml` (74→1,978), `.gitignore` (ingest_review/), `docs/design/quest_expansion_postlaunch_path_v1.md` (new), `docs/design/sourcebook_ingestion_pipeline_v1.md`, `docs/design/sourcebook_grounding_reprioritization_v1.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

---

### 2026-06-13 — Character-load skill robustness (adversarial-sweep hardening) — *fixup*
A break-it-tester sweep of drops 44-50 (all confirmed robust) surfaced 2 character-load crash paths — both fixed defensively (no behavior change for valid data):
- **`from_db_dict` skills loop** (`engine/character.py:882`) had no per-entry guard, so a corrupted `skills` column value (a non-D6 string like `"TRAINED"`) raised an uncaught `ValueError`/`AttributeError` that aborted the whole character load AND leaked raw Python error text to the player (`get_char_obj` is on the `attack`/`+sheet`/HUD path). Now skips a malformed entry with a warning, matching the JSON-decode tolerance already on the blob. **Pre-existing** (predates drop 44); reachable only via DB corruption, but it's the only CRASH+LEAK the sweep found.
- **`Character.has_skill_dice`** (drop 50) raised `AttributeError` on a non-`DicePool` skills value (caught upstream today by `get_powersuit_strength_bonus`, but a footgun for any future caller). Now an `isinstance` guard treats junk as untrained.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_powered_suit.py` (18 — +2 robustness pins) + 548-test character/skill/chargen/powered regression green.
- **Files:** `engine/character.py`, `tests/test_powered_suit.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

### 2026-06-13 — Powered armor: the Powersuit Operation skill + the first exo-suit — *drop 50*
Closes `CRAFT.powered_suit_design` (the other HOOK long-pole). Brian ruled "register a new powersuit skill"; this lands the skill + the first POWERED armor whose servo-assisted Strength bonus reaches combat, gated by proficiency and hard-capped (conservative charter). Unblocked by drop 47's equipment-instance accessor.
- **New skill** (`data/skills.yaml`): `Powersuit Operation` under **Mechanical** (WEG40120's "Powersuit operation", a reaction-capable *Operation skill, sibling of Ground-Vehicle/Walker/Repulsorlift Operation). The registry is data-driven, so it auto-registers with the right governing attribute — `_skill_to_attr` resolves it to `mechanical` (no hardcoded-map edit, unlike the restraints-escape `lifting` case). Spec-free (no Imperial powersuit models in the Clone Wars era). Skill count 75→76.
- **Powered-armor fields** (`engine/weapons.py`): `strength_bonus` (a D6 code) + `powersuit_skill` (the gate flag) on the Weapon dataclass + loader. Default empty/false → every existing armor row is unchanged.
- **The bonus consumer** (`engine/character.py::get_powersuit_strength_bonus`): reads the worn suit's `strength_bonus`, **hard-caps at +1D** (a hypothetical +5D suit still yields +1D — no power creep), and **halves it (floor) for a wearer with no trained Powersuit Operation dice** (the servos fight an untrained pilot). Gated on the armor's `powersuit_skill` flag (so the flag is consumed, not a phantom field) AND a robust skill-presence check via a new `Character.has_skill_dice` helper (tolerates the 0D-skill-isn't-trained case + the underscore-form NPC key dialect — both caught in review). Folded into the combat **soak roll** (`engine/combat.py`): powered armor makes you tankier. Strength-based melee deferred.
- **The first powered suit** (`data/weapons.yaml` + `data/schematics.yaml`): `labor_exo_frame_pl9` "Loader Exo-Frame (PL-9)" — a Clone Wars industrial cargo-loader exo-frame up-armored for the field (+1D STR, +1D+1/+1D+2 soak, -1D Dex). Craftable (Armor Repair / Sela Tarn / diff 20 / composite+metal+rare q70 / 6000cr) — a clear tier above flex-armor. A 6000cr SINK, no new faucet. Integrated weapon mounts stay deferred (lore).
- **Three review findings fixed pre-handback** (invariant-auditor + code-reviewer): the `powersuit_skill` field was a phantom (stored, never read) → now the gate; the skill check counted `DicePool(0,0)` as trained (DicePool has no `__bool__`) → now `has_skill_dice` with `is_zero()`; the underscore-form key was a dead dual-`.get()` → now a canonical scan. All three pinned by new tests.
- **A stale teach-pin** (Sela Tarn's exact armor-schematic set) drifted +1 with the new exo-frame → loosened from exact-match to subset (the structural intent: "all drop-C armor binds to Sela," robust to new Sela schematics).
- **Verified:** `tests/test_powered_suit.py` (16 — skill registers under mechanical, item loads, bonus trained/untrained/0D-untrained/underscore-trained/flag-gated/capped/ordinary-zero/none-zero, combat-soak integration trained>untrained>unpowered, the schematic) + 1129-test armor/weapon/combat/craft/skill regression green (1 stale pin fixed). Invariant-auditor + code-reviewer (all findings fixed).
- **Files:** `data/skills.yaml`, `engine/weapons.py`, `engine/character.py`, `engine/combat.py`, `data/weapons.yaml`, `data/schematics.yaml`, `tests/test_powered_suit.py` (new), `tests/test_craft_p2_gundark_drop_c.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Restraints/handcuffs — verb layer + gates (closes CRAFT.HOOK.restraints) — *drop 49*
Wires the drop-48 engine core to the player surface, completing one of the two HOOK long-poles. A captor can now cuff a defeated/willing prisoner, who's locked out of move/attack/gear until they break free or are released. Mirrors `parser/demolitions_commands.py` (the engine does the work; the parser is a thin front end). Design: `docs/design/restraints_system_design_v1.md`.
- **The binders item** — a craftable consumable (`data/consumables.yaml` + `data/schematics.yaml`: Security skill, taught by Vek Nurren, difficulty 14, metal + composite components, 120cr). A pure SINK (crafted/bought, consumed on a successful cuff) — no credit faucet. Registered in the parity invariant's `NON_STIM_CONSUMERS` so it can't be an inert token.
- **Async orchestration** (`engine/restraints.py`): `attempt_cuff` (resolve target via `match_in_room` → consent/defeat gate → consume binders → persist), `attempt_uncuff` (captor-or-admin authority), `attempt_escape_action`, `set_consent_action`. The gate runs BEFORE consuming binders — a rejected cuff doesn't waste them (pinned).
- **Verbs** (`parser/restraints_commands.py`, registered in `game_server.py`): `cuff <target>` (aliases restrain/bind), `uncuff <target>` (unbind/release), `escape` (struggle), `allowrestrain on|off` (opt-in consent toggle).
- **Gates** — `is_restrained(char)` blocks at 6 sites: `MoveCommand` (can't walk off), `AttackCommand` declaration (can't wield — mirrors the retreat-refusal gate), and the 4 equip verbs Equip/Unequip/Wear/RemoveArmor (can't change gear). Social verbs, `look`, and `escape` stay available (bound ≠ silenced). The gate is a lazy local import + early return, a no-op for the absent-key case (every existing character unchanged).
- **v1 scope (conservative charter, logged):** cuffs PCs only (NPCs have no `attributes` column for persistent state — a future increment); fixed escape DC 15; one escape attempt per command; no damage from being cuffed; release = captor-or-admin only.
- **A craft-parity invariant caught a real gap:** binders, as a craftable consumable, was flagged as an inert token until registered with its real consumer — fixed (the no-phantom-consumer guard working). An almost-shipped phantom component type (`electronics`, not a canonical RESOURCE_TYPE) was caught + corrected to `composite` pre-commit.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_restraints_verbs.py` (16 — the consent/defeat gate across defeated/healthy/consenting, binders consumed-on-success / kept-on-rejection, self/NPC/not-found rejection, uncuff captor/admin/third-party authority, escape persistence, consent toggle) + `test_restraints_engine` (21) + the updated craft-parity test + 297-test move/equip/combat-command regression green. Invariant-auditor CLEAN; smoke-verifier BOOTS CLEAN (227).
- **Files:** `engine/restraints.py`, `parser/restraints_commands.py` (new), `server/game_server.py`, `parser/builtin_commands.py`, `parser/combat_commands.py`, `data/consumables.yaml`, `data/schematics.yaml`, `tests/test_restraints_verbs.py` (new), `tests/test_craft_p2_gundark_drop_a.py`, `docs/design/restraints_system_design_v1.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Restraints/handcuffs — engine core (state model + consent/defeat gate) — *drop 48*
First slice of `CRAFT.HOOK.restraints` (one of the two HOOK long-poles). Builds the persistent-restraint STATE MODEL + the Brian-decided PvP norm as a self-contained, inert engine module — the verb layer (cuff/uncuff/escape/allow-restrain) + the move/attack/equip gates + the binders item land in the follow-up slice that wires this to the player surface (the riskier half — it touches hot command paths — so it ships separately). Design: `docs/design/restraints_system_design_v1.md`.
- **The norm (Brian):** consent/defeat-gated — a PC may be cuffed only if DEFEATED (`wound_level >= INCAPACITATED`) or CONSENTING (`allow restrain` opt-in); a healthy unwilling PC cannot be cuffed (the grief guard). NPCs need no consent.
- **`engine/restraints.py`** (mirrors `engine/buffs.py`): state lives in `attributes.restraint` JSON (`{applied_by, applied_by_id, item_key, escape_difficulty, applied_at}`) — **no schema migration**, and absent = not restrained so every existing character is unchanged. `get_restraint`/`is_restrained`/`can_be_restrained` (the gate)/`apply_restraint`/`release_restraint`/`can_release` (captor-or-admin authority)/`set_consent`/`restraint_consent`/`attempt_escape`. Persistent (survives logout via `save_character`), distinct from the ephemeral combat grapple (`Combatant.restraint`).
- **Escape = WEG Strength, funnel-routed.** `attempt_escape` rolls `perform_skill_check(char, "lifting", difficulty)` — `lifting` is the Strength-GOVERNED skill, so it's a faithful Strength roll. **A test caught a real bug here**: routing through `"strength"` directly mis-maps (the skill→attr table sends the bare attribute name to the perception default → a 2D roll ignoring the prisoner's real Strength). Fixed to `lifting`. Conservative: fixed DC 15 (Moderate), one attempt per command, no auto-progress (retunable when T3.19 lands).
- **Balance (conservative charter):** no cuffing healthy unwilling PCs, no damage from being cuffed, release = captor-or-admin only (capture stays meaningful), binders are a pure item sink (no credit faucet).
- **Inert:** no callers yet (the verb slice wires it) — the same producer-without-consumer-is-authorized pattern as the ambient Phase 0 scaffolding; the design doc authorizes it.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_restraints_engine.py` (21 — the consent/defeat gate across healthy/defeated/mortally-wounded/wounded-not-incap/consenting/NPC/already-restrained/malformed, apply-read-release round-trip, JSON-string + dict shape preservation = logout survival, release authority, consent toggle, escape uses the stored difficulty + rolls real Strength). AST clean.
- **Files:** `engine/restraints.py` (new), `tests/test_restraints_engine.py` (new), `docs/design/restraints_system_design_v1.md` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Equipment-instance accessor (Stage 1) + 2 live consumer-bug fixes — *drop 47*
Chips the `TD.EQUIPMENT_CHARACTER_HOLDS_KEYS_NOT_INSTANCES` debt with the lowest-risk slice, chosen from a full 5-surface seam map (98 read/write sites classified: only 4 genuinely need the instance, 2 of which drop 19/44 already thread). The Character holds equipped_weapon/worn_armor as bare KEY strings while the canonical DB column is per-slot ItemInstance JSON — so any consumer wanting condition/quality/crafter had to re-parse the JSON, which BLOCKS `CRAFT.powered_suit_design` + OBS armor Option C. **Additive accessor, NOT a field-type change** (the seam map found 56 key-only consumers that a type change would break) — "extend, don't add": reuses `read_equipment`, touches zero string contracts.
- **The accessor** (`engine/character.py`): new `_equipment_slots` field cached in `from_db_dict` from the `read_equipment` call it already makes (and now in `from_npc_sheet` too, so armed NPCs aren't None); read-only properties `equipped_weapon_inst` / `worn_armor_inst` expose the full ItemInstance. The bare-key fields + all 56 key-only consumers are untouched. Documented as a **load-time snapshot** (mirrors drop 44's `armor_soak_pips`).
- **Bug fix 1 — web HUD loadout was always blank** (`server/session.py::_build_loadout`): it read `name`/`damage`/`type` off the equipment JSON, but that's the `ItemInstance.to_dict()` shape (`key`/`condition`/`quality`/`crafter`) — those keys never existed, so the sidebar showed nothing. Now resolves the **name from the weapon registry by key**. (Emits ONLY `name` — the field the SPA actually consumes; richer condition/quality display is deferred to a drop that ships its SPA consumer too, to avoid a phantom-producer.)
- **Bug fix 2 — intel scans always reported "Unarmed"** (`engine/espionage.py::generate_scan_result`): it read `equipped_weapon`/`worn_armor` off the char DICT, but those are Character *attributes*, never dict columns — dead reads. Now reads the slot keys via `equipment_keys(char["equipment"])`, so espionage reports the real loadout.
- **Staleness fix (code-review)**: the 6 equip/wear/unequip/remove/repair/sell verbs mutate `char["equipment"]` but didn't drop the session's cached Character object, so its load-time snapshots (`_equipment_slots` AND drop 44's `armor_soak_pips`) went stale mid-session. Added `ctx.session.invalidate_char_obj()` after each — **also fixes drop 44's latent `armor_soak_pips` staleness**.
- **Phantom-producer fix (invariant-review)**: the first cut of `_build_loadout` emitted condition/quality/crafter/damage/type/protection_* that no SPA module reads — trimmed to the consumed `name` only.
- **Unblocks** `CRAFT.powered_suit_design` + OBS armor Option C — their rich-instance consumers can now read `char.worn_armor_inst.quality` instead of re-parsing JSON (their own design forks still gate the build). Bare-key retirement (Stage 3) stays deferred to T3.20.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_equipment_instance_accessor.py` (19 — accessor across all shapes + None safety + from_npc_sheet + the `_build_loadout` and espionage regression locks + bare-keys-untouched + armor_soak_pips coexistence) + 124-test equipment/inventory/session-HUD/espionage regression + the str-shape tests (equipment-untangle, gundark, social-force) stay green (additive contract). Reviewed: invariant-auditor + code-reviewer (both findings fixed). AST clean.
- **Files:** `engine/character.py`, `server/session.py`, `engine/espionage.py`, `parser/builtin_commands.py`, `tests/test_equipment_instance_accessor.py` (new), `tests/test_cities_phase4b.py` (mock completeness), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Ambient NPC life — Phase 0 DB scaffolding (pre-launch, inert) — *drop 46*
Lands the PRE-LAUNCH database scaffolding for T3.22 (ambient NPC life — idle-Ollama background world sim). Per the design doc (`ambient_npc_life_design_v1.md` §5-6) and Brian's live-safety requirement, the schema lands NOW (inert) so the post-launch sim build never migrates a live, populated DB. This drop is the ONLY part of the feature that touches the live DB, and it does so while it's safe. Nothing reads these tables yet — the sim (Phases 1-4) is post-launch.
- **Schema v44** (`db/database.py`): two new tables, in BOTH `SCHEMA_SQL` (fresh installs) AND `MIGRATIONS[44]` (existing-DB upgrade), all `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` (idempotent, lowest-risk migration class — new empty tables, no ALTER on a hot table). `npc_ambient_state` (per-NPC goal/movement/activity runtime state, `npc_id` PK → `npcs(id)`, `current_room_id`/`dest_room_id` → `rooms(id)`, + `idx_npc_ambient_room`) and `npc_ambient_relationship` (NPC-NPC affinity, composite PK). Both carry a JSON `extra TEXT DEFAULT '{}'` future-proof blank — the SQLite-idiomatic "blank space" (new fields go into JSON with zero migration, like `characters.attributes`), per Brian's future-proofing instinct. Ambient config rides the existing `npcs.ai_config_json` (no schema change; `ambient_enabled` absent/false = every existing NPC unchanged — the key live-safety property).
- **Inert accessor stubs** (`db/database.py`, mirror the `cp_ticks` trio): `ambient_state_get` / `ambient_state_ensure_row` (INSERT OR IGNORE) / `ambient_state_update` (allowlisted columns) / `ambient_state_in_room` (the co-location query, uses the index). **NO callers yet** — shipped now so Phase 1 is pure feature code against present tables + accessors, with no DB-layer change racing a live player. A test asserts this inertness (no production reader).
- **Live-safety verified:** invariant-auditor CLEAN (confirmed the deliberate producer-without-consumer is the correct authorized pre-launch shape, not a phantom); smoke-verifier BOOTS CLEAN (227 smoke tests — `Database.initialize()` applies the v44 migration without error, full boot sequence clean).
- **Verified:** `tests/test_t3_22_ambient_life_phase0.py` (13 — version bump, migration 44 registered, both table shapes incl. the `extra` blank, the index, the v43→v44 upgrade path, idempotent re-run, accessor round-trip + FK enforcement + allowlist, the inertness guard) + 110-test schema/migration regression green. AST clean.
- **Files:** `db/database.py`, `tests/test_t3_22_ambient_life_phase0.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — xdist full-suite triage: 4 failures triaged, 3 stale-tests + 1 real silent-except fixed — *triage session*
Ran the full pytest suite under pytest-xdist (`-n auto`, 12 workers, ~8,800 items) per `HANDOFF_xdist_suite_triage_2026-06-13.md`, then triaged each failure real-bug-vs-bad-test single-threaded before touching anything. **4 distinct failures; 3 were tests drifted from correct/intended engine behavior, 1 was a real (minor) production silent-except.** Final suite: `8793 passed, 1 failed` — the lone red is purely two UNTRACKED guide-tool strays from the parallel guides-rework session (not this session's lane; flagged for Brian). No engine logic was wrong.
- **F1 — `test_drop_f8c2a_chain_anchors::test_every_chain_npc_is_authored` (STALE TEST).** Checked chain NPC refs against a SINGLE roster file; the T5-questline arc (drops 34–35) legitimately authored 5 master-trainer NPCs in a SECOND roster (`npcs_drop_b_t5_trainers.yaml`), era-wired and consumed by the roster-agnostic runtime talk_to_npc lookup. Producer+manifest+consumer all present → not a phantom. **Fix:** new `_all_authored_npc_names()` unions NPC names across every `era.yaml::content_refs.npcs` roster; coverage now checks that union. `test_no_extra_npcs` left file-scoped (correct per-file identity check). 26/26 green.
- **F2 — `spa/test_map_label_lod::test_zoom_reveals_more_rooms` (TEST BUG).** Production `_labelRoomsForNavigation` is correct (verified by running the brace-matched fn directly under node+jsdom: far-zoom → all 6 rooms). The test read `far` AFTER an idempotency re-run on the same SVG at zoom:1 collapsed the label set back to fit-3. **Fix:** snapshot `farLabels` immediately after the zoom-2.5 call, before the re-run. 3/3 green.
- **F3 — `spa/test_m3_substrate_hybrid::...skips_procedural` (STALE PIN).** Substrate mode emits 10 children; test pinned 9. The 10th is `L_SubstrateRooms`, the intended tier≤1 tactical click-target layer that replaces the skipped procedural `L_Buildings` so click-to-walk works under a painting. Engine + the stale `9` landed in the SAME squash commit but were never run together (no jsdom → always skipped). **Fix:** 9→10 with attribution + added the layer's first positive coverage (the `substrate-rooms` group + its 2 non-street click targets). 8/8 green.
- **F4 — `test_session38::test_no_silent_except_pass_in_production` (1 REAL + 2 not-in-lane).** REAL: `parser/commissary_commands.py` had a bare `except: pass` swallowing a best-effort `shop_state` web push with no logging (`:197`), plus two comment-masked siblings the scanner missed (`:117`, `:146`). **Fixed all three** to `log.debug("[commissary] <list|buy|sell> shop_state push failed", exc_info=True)`, matching the bounty/inventory/crafting best-effort idiom. NOT-IN-LANE: two UNTRACKED guides-session tools (`guide_lint.py:39`, `split_guide_dev_track.py:35`) use the benign `sys.std*.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")` console-bootstrap idiom; per Brian, flagged not touched — logged as `OBS.silent_except_invariant_reconfigure_carveout`.
- **Process findings (recorded for the next triage):** (1) the box had two competing `-n auto` masters + ~20 hung 0%-CPU execnet zombies from reaped background runs — swept before launching one clean run (memory: `xdist-orphan-process-swarm`). (2) `pytest.ini addopts` carries `-x`, so the first run stopped at 3 and hid F4; any triage run must strip it (`-o addopts="" --maxfail=N`). (3) F2/F3 surfaced for the first time because drop 26 made `node_modules` repo-local-resolvable → jsdom now present → previously-skipped spa tests run.
- **Verified:** full `-n auto` suite `8793 passed, 1 failed (the 2 untracked strays only), 28 skipped, 5 xfailed, 368 subtests passed` in ~5min; all 4 affected files green single-threaded; AST/JSON clean.
- **Files:** `tests/test_drop_f8c2a_chain_anchors.py`, `tests/spa/test_map_label_lod.py`, `tests/spa/test_m3_substrate_hybrid.py`, `parser/commissary_commands.py`, `TODO.json` (design-call append), `CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/design/HANDOFF_xdist_suite_triage_RESULTS_2026-06-13.md` (new). **Not committed** — TODO/CHANGELOG are shared + dirty from concurrent sessions; Brian commits.

### 2026-06-13 — Crafted consumable quality → potency (consumable half) — *drop 45*
Builds the CONSUMABLE half of `CRAFT.armor_consumable_quality_combat`, closing the last of the `OBS.quality_and_boosts` decoration gap (weapons drop-19, armor drop-44, consumables here). Crafted consumable quality was stored at craft time but discarded — only shown in the delivery message — so a q95 stimpack was mechanically identical to a vendor one. Design + balance analysis: `docs/design/consumable_quality_potency_v1.md` (written this session per Brian's "do a design doc first if you need it, don't stall"). Resolves the `CRAFT.consumable_quality_potency_model` fork on its recommended defaults.
- **Funnel (`engine/items.py::crafted_consumable_potency_pips`)** — TIGHTER than the weapon/armor band: cap **+1** (not +2), because a stim buff base is only +1D=3 pips, so even one pip is +33% and a +2 would push a q95 stim toward the next catalog tier (power creep). The whole good band (70-100) yields exactly +1; quality buys RELIABILITY of hitting +1, not magnitude. Takes a quality INT (consumables aren't ItemInstances — they store a per-key quality), reuses `_quality_band_pips`. Band: ≤49=-1, 50-69=0, 70-100=+1. Fail-open 0; vendor q50=0.
- **Storage migration (`engine/buffs.py`)** — consumables move from a bare int to `{"count", "quality"}`. New `_normalize_consumable_entry` coerces BOTH generations (legacy int → q50 vendor baseline; new dict as-is) so no DB migration script is needed — the read coerces lazily, the next write upgrades the shape (mirrors the equipment-column tolerance). `has_consumable`/`get_consumable_count`/`consume_consumable` route through it; `consume_consumable` PRESERVES the per-key quality on decrement and **stays a `bool`** (so its callers — `engine/breaching.py`, `parser/medical_commands.py` — keep their truthiness contract; no signature ripple). New `get_consumable_quality(char, key)` reads the per-key quality (the medic reads it before consuming). [Refines the design doc's "change consume_consumable's return type" to a separate reader — strictly less blast radius.]
- **Delivery write (`parser/crafting_commands.py`)** — persists quality alongside count, **max-on-recraft** (a better craft always upgrades the stack; quality is per-key since stims are fungible).
- **Potency hooks (`parser/medical_commands.py`)** — STIMS: scale the buff's `stat_modifiers` by the pip (floored at 0 so a shoddy stim is weaker but never a debuff) via an `add_buff(..., stat_modifiers=<scaled>)` override; q50→0 pip→template unchanged (vendor parity). MEDPACS: heal magnitude stays DISCRETE (1/2/1) — quality instead eases the First-Aid roll (lower effective difficulty = more reliable application), so a fine medpac applies cleanly more often without a balance-warping bigger heal; no double-dip (stims get magnitude, medpacs get reliability).
- **Power-creep guard (pinned):** a q95 stimpack reaches +1D+1 (4 pips) — strictly below `adrenaline_shot`'s +2D (6) vendor base, so the tier ladder holds. Asserted in `test_consumable_quality_potency.py::TestStimBuffScaling::test_q95_stim_below_next_tier`.
- **Migration regression:** the old `test_srb1b` assertion that post-consume storage is a bare int, and the `economy_progression` smoke assertion reading `consumables.medpac` as an int, were updated to read the count via `_normalize_consumable_entry` (tolerant of both shapes) — a legitimate test-update for the by-design storage change, not an engine fix.
- **Two code-review bugs caught + fixed pre-handback** (code-reviewer fan-out): (1) **`engine/buffs.py::add_buff` refresh path** silently dropped a `stat_modifiers` override when a buff of the same type already existed — so re-applying a higher-quality stim over an active one (the overdose path; all stims are `max_stacks=1` → always the refresh branch) kept the OLD magnitude. Now applies an EXPLICIT `stat_modifiers` override to the existing buff (template defaults still don't reset on a plain re-apply). (2) **delivery write used `int(quality)`** which truncated the `resolve_craft` float, so a craft displayed as "quality 70" (from 69.9) stored 69 → 0 pips instead of the +1 the player was shown; now `round(quality)` to match the displayed value and the band boundary. Both pinned by new tests (`TestAddBuffOverrideOnReapply`, `TestQualityRoundingAtBoundary`).
- **Verified:** `tests/test_consumable_quality_potency.py` (43 — funnel band/cap, normalizer both shapes incl. bool-not-count guard, quality-aware helpers, max-on-recraft, stim scaling + the tier-ladder invariant, + the 2 review-bug regressions) + `test_srb1b_stim_consumable_wiring` (updated) + `test_srb1_medic_stim` + `test_craft_p2_gundark_drop_a` (medpac heal UNCHANGED) + all `add_buff` consumers incl. `test_t2_10c_overdose_incap` green (132 combined) + AST clean + full xdist suite green modulo the 1 known not-mine guides-tool stray.
- **Files:** `engine/items.py`, `engine/buffs.py`, `parser/crafting_commands.py`, `parser/medical_commands.py`, `tests/test_consumable_quality_potency.py` (new), `tests/test_srb1b_stim_consumable_wiring.py`, `tests/smoke/scenarios/economy_progression.py`, `docs/design/consumable_quality_potency_v1.md` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

---

### 2026-06-13 — Crafted armor quality → combat soak (armor half) — *drop 44*
Builds the ARMOR half of `CRAFT.armor_consumable_quality_combat` (Brian-greenlit: "quality should absolutely matter"). Closes the armor side of the half-closed `OBS.quality_and_boosts` decoration gap that the crafting-integration review flagged: a crafted q95 vest used to soak EXACTLY like a vendor/q40 one because `get_armor_protection` read only the bare `worn_armor` KEY (the instance quality was discarded by `from_db_dict`). Mirrors the ratified Drop-19 weapon precedent (`crafted_combat_pips`), hard-capped to forbid power creep.
- **Funnel (`engine/items.py::crafted_armor_soak_pips`)** — the armor sibling of `crafted_combat_pips`, in the same documented "ONLY places crafted quality becomes a combat number" funnel block. Reuses `_quality_band_pips` VERBATIM (≤49=-1, 50-69=0, 70-89=+1, 90-100=+2) so armor and weapons share one band boundary. Cap `max(-1, min(2, ...))` — naturally +2 (not the weapon's +3=+1D), because armor has NO experiment-mod axis in the registry (`weapons.yaml` carries only `protection_energy`/`protection_physical`, no `protection_mod`), so there's no boost term. Returns int (one combat axis: soak), vs the weapon's (dmg, acc) tuple. Fail-open: 0 for None/non-instance; vendor q50 = 0.
- **Producer (`engine/character.py::from_db_dict`)** — computes the soak pip ONCE at load, where the equipment JSON is still in hand (it's discarded past this point), and caches it as a new primitive `Character.armor_soak_pips: int = 0`. Mirrors the Drop-19 weapon-pip isolation (combat never re-reads equipment). Switched the equipment read from `equipment_keys` to a single `read_equipment` call (keys derived from the same slots — no double-parse). `from_db_dict` is the UNIVERSAL Character construction path (players via `session.get_char_obj`, DB NPCs via `Character.from_db_dict`), so one producer site covers every combat participant; `invalidate_char_obj` after `save_character` means re-equipping recomputes.
- **Consumer (`engine/combat.py::_apply_damage`)** — folds `target.armor_soak_pips` into `armor_pool` BEFORE Strength-add and BEFORE the wound penalty, only when the armor already protects against this damage type (`not armor_pool.is_zero()`), so quality AMPLIFIES existing protection rather than conjuring soak for a damage type the armor doesn't cover (e.g. a +pip on an energy-only vest does not soak a melee hit). `DicePool + int` carries pips (3 = +1D). The structured `soak_armor_pool` inspector event auto-reflects the delta (folded before emit). `get_armor_protection` itself is UNCHANGED — so `test_craft_p2_gundark_drop_c`'s registry soak-row pins stay green (the delta lives in combat, not the registry read).
- **Cap rationale:** +2/floor -1 mirrors the weapon quality band Brian already ratified; soak adds alongside Strength so the conservative band is the power-creep-safe choice.
- **Consumable half deferred** to a follow-up drop with its own design doc (the potency model is a genuine power-creep balance fork — pip-delta-on-base vs quality-factor — plus medpac-scaling and a bare-int→quality-dict storage migration; Brian: "do a design doc/analysis first if you need it, don't stall").
- **Verified:** `tests/test_craft_armor_soak_quality.py` (25 — funnel bands/cap/fail-open, producer captures pip from equipment JSON across all 3 historical shapes, consumer q95>q40 soak incl. physical, registry path unchanged) + `test_obs_quality_combat` (50) + `test_craft_p2_gundark_drop_c` (20) regression green + AST clean.
- **Files:** `engine/items.py`, `engine/character.py`, `engine/combat.py`, `tests/test_craft_armor_soak_quality.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

---

### 2026-06-13 — T5 ship-part effects verified + crafting-review integration — *drop 43*
Closes T2.DEF.t5_ship_part_items and integrates the safe findings from the parallel crafting-integration review (`HANDOFF_crafting_integration_review_2026-06-13.md`), per the main-session watch protocol (integrate non-conflicting, STOP+log real forks).
- **T5 ship parts — verified ALREADY WORKING, NOT rebuilt.** The TODO's "items are inert in combat/space" was STALE: the full loop exists (CRAFT.P0.3/P0.4) — `output_type: component` craft → `type: ship_component` item carrying quality → `+ship/install` (`_install_mod`: owner+docked-gated, reads the component quality via `_quality_factor`, caps via `_MOD_MAX_*`, install skill check) → `get_effective_stats` applies the quality-scaled boost to live ship stats. The review's "thread the INSTANCE quality, don't key off the bare key" trap is already handled correctly here (unlike the armor bug). Building a new install mechanic would've been a redundant parallel system — so I verified instead of rebuilt. `tests/test_t5_ship_part_effects.py` (5) locks in craft-shape → install → quality-scaled effective-stat boost end-to-end, with a regression guard that quality is genuinely consulted (not decoration).
- **Review item 2 integrated (contained, safe):** dropped the phantom `stat_note` in `engine/crafting.py::resolve_craft` that promised "a minor/significant combat bonus" off `QUALITY_TIERS.stat_bonus` — which NO combat code reads (the real crafted-weapon bonus is the independent `items.py` pip path). Stopped rendering a promise the engine doesn't honor. No test asserted the old strings.
- **Review items 1 & 3 LOGGED as design calls (Brian's, not built):** `CRAFT.quality_combat_read_armor_consumables` (item 3 — the balance-sensitive fork: should crafted armor-soak / consumable-potency scale with quality like weapons do? gates 2 HIGH-collision combat-file wirings; bundle armor with the equipment-instance migration) + `CRAFT.rare_resource_no_vendor` (item 1 — confirm the rare-material no-vendor gate is intentional). Neither blind-integrated (HIGH combat-file collision + balance judgment).
- **No conflict with any shipped drop** — the review verified the crafting/harvest/economy backbone (incl. the t5 trainers, commissary sellback, breaching consumable) is clean.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_t5_ship_part_effects.py` (5) + 99-test crafting regression + smoke green.
- **Files:** `engine/crafting.py`, `tests/test_t5_ship_part_effects.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Breaching obstacle placement: world-data seeding + authored obstacles — *drop 42*
Gives the breach verb (drop 40) real authored targets, resolving the placement half of CRAFT.breaching_obstacle_placement (Brian: "seed objects now, exits later"). Adds the FIRST object-seeding-at-world-build path (objects were runtime-only before — corpses, drops, admin).
- **`engine/world_writer.py::_write_breachables`** (called from `write_world_bundle` after exits): for each room with an optional `breachables:` list in its YAML (additive — captured in `room.raw`), creates a `breachable` object (type/name/room_id/data.breach_difficulty/data.reveal). **Idempotent** — dedup by (type, name, room_id) so a rebuild/restart never piles up duplicate obstacles (mirrors create_exit's no-op-on-duplicate). `owner_id=NULL` (world-placed; `owner_id=0` would violate the objects→characters FK — caught by the real-DB test). Failure-tolerant per entry.
- **3 authored obstacles** (additive YAML, 8 lines each, 0 deleted — map-safe), each diegetic to a room whose description already implies a seal, all in Contested/Wilds zones: Geonosis `barracks_armory` "Mark-locked armory grate" (diff 22), Nar Shaddaa `warrens_collapsed_plaza` "Rubble-choked Evocii doorway" (diff 18), Tatooine `jundland_hidden_cave` "Tumbled-boulder screen" (diff 15). A player can now craft a breaching_charge and breach a real target end-to-end.
- **Deferred (logged):** gating real MAP EXITS with breach (the map-entangled option) stays a future increment per Brian.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_world_writer.py::TestBreachables` (2 — the 3 obstacles seed with correct difficulty/reveal against the REAL CW world in an in-memory DB; re-seed writes 0 / no duplicates) + 65-test world-loader/writer/breaching regression + smoke green.
- **Files:** `engine/world_writer.py`, `data/worlds/clone_wars/planets/{geonosis,nar_shaddaa,tatooine}.yaml`, `tests/test_world_writer.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Harvest per-region skill override (Republic-tech salvage → Search) — *drop 41*
Resolves CRAFT.harvest_skill_flavor (Brian's call: per-region override). Harvest defaults to wilderness Survival, but salvage regions yield tech, not flora/fauna — recovering usable Republic tech from the Coruscant Underworld reads as Search (scavenging/recovering), not Survival.
- **`engine/harvest.py`:** new `_REGION_HARVEST_SKILL` map (region slug → skill, default `survival`) + `harvest_skill_for_region(slug)` helper, mirroring the existing `_REGION_SCAVENGE_BONUS` region-keyed table. `coruscant_underworld → search`. The active-harvest skill check now rolls the region's skill (difficulty unchanged at Easy 6). Pure data add — a new region is one row, no other code change. (Search chosen over a raw Technical-attribute roll: it's a real skill and reads as "scavenging".)
- **Verified:** `tests/test_harvest_skill_override.py` (4 — default Survival, Coruscant→Search, case-insensitive, override-is-canonical) + 97-test harvest regression (test_syn6a + test_syn6c) green. Engine-internal (no command/boot/schema surface).
- **Files:** `engine/harvest.py`, `tests/test_harvest_skill_override.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

---

### 2026-06-13 — Breaching charges (breach verb + demolitions check) — *drop 40*
Builds the breaching half of CRAFT.mines_breaching_split (Brian greenlit the split — build breaching, defer placed mines). A `breach <target>` verb blows open a sealed obstacle with a single-use breaching charge and a Demolitions check vs the obstacle's difficulty. Safe by design (breaches obstacles, not people — no blast-on-players); placed proximity mines remain a separate deferred subsystem.
- **Engine core** (`engine/breaching.py::attempt_breach`, mirrors `engine/housing.py::attempt_force_door`): finds a `breachable` room object (type `breachable`, `data.breach_difficulty`), requires + consumes a breaching charge (via `engine.buffs.consume_consumable` — the charge is a crafted consumable in `attributes.consumables`, spent on the ATTEMPT whether or not it succeeds, grenade single-use precedent), runs `perform_skill_check("demolitions", difficulty)`, and on success deletes the obstacle and reveals its `data.reveal` flavor. Failure-tolerant throughout (`find_breachable` handles no-target / ambiguous / mismatch).
- **`breach` command** (`parser/demolitions_commands.py`, new; registered in `game_server.py` + the reachability-test registry): thin front end + a room broadcast of the blast on success.
- **Craftable breaching charge:** `breaching_charge` schematic (Demolitions, diff 17, taught by Kayson) → `output_type: consumable` + a `data/consumables.yaml` identity row (category `ordnance`). The three-way consumable-parity invariant (`test_craft_p2_gundark_drop_a`) was extended with a `NON_STIM_CONSUMERS` allow-set documenting where the non-stim mechanic lives (`engine/breaching.py`) — so the "no inert consumable tokens" rule stays honest while allowing a non-stim consumer.
- **No blast-on-players, no new faucet** (the charge is a crafted sink-side item). **Placed mines deferred.**
- **Open call logged** — `CRAFT.breaching_obstacle_placement`: no world-data object-seeding path exists today, so breachable obstacles are admin/builder-placeable now but not yet authored at scale; the seeding-vs-exit-gating placement path is a pending design call. The MECHANIC is complete + tested regardless.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_breaching.py` (9 — find_breachable matching, charge gate, consume-on-attempt for success AND failure, obstacle deletion, no-obstacle/mismatch paths) + the consumable-parity invariant + reachability invariant + smoke green.
- **Files:** `engine/breaching.py` (new), `parser/demolitions_commands.py` (new), `server/game_server.py`, `data/schematics.yaml`, `data/consumables.yaml`, `tests/test_breaching.py` (new), `tests/test_craft_p2_gundark_drop_a.py`, `tests/test_chain_corpus_reachability_invariant.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — Director CW faction-order mapping layer — *drop 39*
Resolves DIRECTOR.faction_model_cw_mapping. The LLM Director's faction_order boundary validated against GCW ORG codes `{empire, rebel, hutt, bh_guild}` (`engine/director.py:964`), so it could never issue era-correct Clone Wars orders — `_apply_faction_order`'s `get_organization(code)` lookup missed every time (republic/cis/hutt_cartel/bounty_hunters_guild are the real CW orgs). Per the design call this is a MAPPING LAYER at the two boundaries ONLY; the sanctioned ZoneState zone-tone AXIS keys (imperial/rebel/criminal/independent) are left entirely untouched.
- **Order boundary:** new `normalize_faction_order_code` accepts a CW org code (pass-through) OR a legacy GCW alias (`_GCW_TO_CW_FACTION` forward-map, mirroring `organizations.yaml`'s `legacy_rewicker.factions`), normalizing to the CW code the org system knows before `_apply_faction_order`. Garbage / axis-only keys → "" (skipped). Replaced the dead hardcoded `VALID_FACTION_CODES` frozenset.
- **Digest boundary:** `compile_digest` now carries `faction_order_codes` (the 4 CW org codes) + a `faction_axis_to_org` legend (imperial→republic, rebel→cis, criminal→hutt_cartel, independent→independent), so the LLM issues CW-correct codes up front. `zone_influence` STILL keys on the untouched axis labels (the ZoneState math is unchanged).
- **Era note:** the new `empire`/`rebel` strings are GCW→CW *mapping keys* (CLAUDE.md-sanctioned, era-mapping-key exempt), confirmed by the 178-test era-cleanness sweep staying green.
- **Closes tech_debt `TD.DIRECTOR_FACTION_MODEL_GCW`** (the capability gap; the full internal re-key stays deferred indefinitely — internal-only, not a bug).
- **Verified:** `tests/test_director_cw_faction_mapping.py` (6 — normalizer pass-through/forward-map/case/garbage/axis-not-an-order-code + the digest legend + untouched-axis pin) + 80-director regression + 178 era-cleanness + smoke green.
- **Files:** `engine/director.py`, `tests/test_director_cw_faction_mapping.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

---

### 2026-06-13 — Commissary sellback model (anti-laundering refund + bind-to-channel) — *drop 38*
Resolves the ECON.commissary_sellback design call in full (all 3 pieces — Brian ratified incl. same-faction trade). Faction-issued / commissary gear can no longer be bought at the requisition discount and resold on the open market for profit (the laundering loop), and it stays within its issuing faction.
- **Piece 1 — vendor refusal (the anti-laundering lock):** `engine/items.py::npc_refuses_buyback` now also refuses any `faction_issued` item (was crafted-quality-only). Works on both ItemInstance and plain inventory dicts (new `_item_attr` shim). Ordinary vendors hand faction gear back. (The vendor-droid path already refused it — this closes the NPC-vendor `sell` path too.)
- **Piece 2 — the commissary refund channel:** `engine/commissary.py::sell_commissary` + `+commissary sell <key>`. Faction gear sells back ONLY to the issuing faction's commissary, refunding `COMMISSARY_SELLBACK_RATE` (50%) of the item's requisition cost via the metered `commissary_sellback` faucet — BY CONSTRUCTION smaller than the `commissary_purchase` sink that created it, so the buy→sellback round trip is a strict NET LOSS (pinned by `TestNoLaunderingLoop`). The requisition cost is now stamped on the item at purchase (`requisition_cost`), with a stock-price fallback for legacy items. Refund-safe ordering (remove item, then credit; restore on credit failure). Channel-bound: you sell Republic gear to the Republic commissary, not the Hutts'.
- **Piece 3 — bind-to-channel transfer:** `engine/items.py::faction_bound_transfer_blocked` gates `give` (PC→PC) and `trade` so faction-issued gear only transfers to a SAME-FACTION member (intra-faction redistribution is lore-apt + laundering-neutral; outsiders refused). NPC hand-off unaffected (consumed). Items without a `faction_code` fall back to not-blocked (the vendor lock is the real laundering guard).
- **Faucet/sink:** the only new tag is the `commissary_sellback` faucet, paired with + smaller than the existing `commissary_purchase` sink. Routed through `adjust_credits` (auto-visible on `@economy`).
- **Verified:** `tests/test_commissary_sellback.py` (18 — all 3 pieces incl. the net-loss invariant, wrong-channel refusal, legacy stock fallback, refund-safe restore) + 110-test commissary/buyback/vendor-sell/give/trade/builtin regression green + smoke.
- **Files:** `engine/items.py`, `engine/commissary.py`, `parser/commissary_commands.py`, `parser/builtin_commands.py`, `tests/test_commissary_sellback.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

---

### 2026-06-13 — World-event flag consumers (3 of 5): distress + hutt_auction + brawl — *drop 37*
Completes the WORLDEVENT.flag_effect_consumers gap — all 5 dead world-event FLAG effects now have thin consumers over existing systems (the design call is now fully resolved). Same pattern throughout: a pure modulator (unit-tested without the manager) read at one existing seam.
- **`distress_active` (DISTRESS_SIGNAL → mission board):** `engine/missions.py::distress_mission_bonus` (+30% emergency pay, `DISTRESS_REWARD_BONUS`). In `generate_mission`, while the signal is live, the rolled type is forced to MEDICAL (a distress call IS a medical/rescue job), the reward gets the premium, and the title becomes "DISTRESS: <dest>". Mirrors the existing Director-alert-level bias in `_pick_type`.
- **`hutt_auction` (HUTT_AUCTION → rep-gated rare purchase):** per the design call's "SIMPLIFY to a rep-gated purchase, NOT bidding." `engine/world_events.py::hutt_auction_purchase_allowed` (gate: active AND hutt_cartel rep ≥ the event's `criminal_rep_gate`, default 30) + `apply_hutt_auction_markup` (+40%). Wired into the buy command (`parser/space_commands.py`): while a Hutt auction is live, an eligible criminal can buy a normally-UNSTOCKED item (the ONLY bypass of the market-segmentation gate) at the auction markup. Fail-closed — any error falls through to the normal refusal, never accidentally opens the gate. (`char` assignment moved up a few lines so the async rep check can live in the refusal branch; no behavior change to the existing path.)
- **`brawl_active` (CANTINA_BRAWL → the d66 cantina table):** `engine/cantina_encounters.py::roll_cantina_encounter(brawl_active=…)` forces the barroom-brawl beat (code 12) on a random `+cantina` roll while the event is live (the brawl is literally happening). Explicit `+cantina <code>` is unaffected.
- **No new faucets:** distress rides the existing mission-reward faucet (bonus on an existing payout); hutt_auction is a SINK (a premium-priced purchase); brawl is pure flavor-table steering.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_worldevent_flag_consumers.py` now 26 (all 5 flags — pure modulators incl. edge/fail-closed cases + manager-driven flag paths incl. `generate_mission` forcing MEDICAL) + the market-segmentation source-pin made robust (order invariant, not a brittle char-window) + 268-mission/bounty/scene/market/vendor regression green.
- **Files:** `engine/missions.py`, `engine/world_events.py`, `engine/cantina_encounters.py`, `parser/space_commands.py`, `parser/scene_commands.py`, `tests/test_worldevent_flag_consumers.py`, `tests/test_econ_market_segmentation.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

---

### 2026-06-13 — World-event flag consumers (2 of 5): rare_vendor + krayt_bounty — *drop 36*
First two of the five dead world-event FLAG effects get thin consumers, per the WORLDEVENT.flag_effect_consumers design call (build one flag per increment; extend-don't-add over existing systems, mirroring the contraband_scan precedent — read the flag at one seam, modulate a deterministic outcome).
- **`rare_vendor` (MERCHANT_ARRIVAL):** new pure modulator `engine/world_events.py::apply_rare_vendor_discount` (15% off the pre-haggle base, `RARE_VENDOR_DISCOUNT` tunable, floored at 1). Consumed at the buy-command seam (`parser/space_commands.py` BuyCommand, right after `base_price` is set so the haggle + faction mods work from the lower base); surfaces a "[A rare merchant's arrival has cut prices…]" line on purchase. Failure-tolerant (flag-lookup hiccup → unchanged price).
- **`krayt_bounty` (KRAYT_SIGHTING):** new pure helper `engine/bounty_board.py::krayt_upgrade_tier` bumps a newly-posted contract one tier toward SUPERIOR (a krayt sighting = a richer, deadlier hunt). Consumed in `generate_bounty` right after `_pick_tier()`. SUPERIOR contracts unchanged; failure-tolerant.
- **Both follow the pure-function pattern** so each modulator unit-tests without the manager, and the manager-driven flag path is tested by activating the real event and asserting the flag drives the modulation. No new faucet (rare_vendor is a SINK reduction; krayt_bounty rides the existing bounty reward scaling via the upgraded tier — the bounty faucet is unchanged in mechanism).
- **Remaining 3** (own future increments, per the one-per-drop call): `brawl_active` (CANTINA_BRAWL — needs a combat/NPC-spawn seam), `distress_active` (DISTRESS_SIGNAL — a mission-injection seam), `hutt_auction` (HUTT_AUCTION — a rep-gated rare-purchase seam). These touch generation/spawn more invasively and each wants its own small drop.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_worldevent_flag_consumers.py` (12 — both pure modulators incl. edge cases + both manager-driven flag paths via real `activate_event`) + 202-bounty + 25-worldevent/market regression green.
- **Files:** `engine/world_events.py`, `engine/bounty_board.py`, `parser/space_commands.py`, `tests/test_worldevent_flag_consumers.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-12/13 — Claude Code tooling additions (parallel tooling session) — *non-drop, config/agents only*
Workflow/tooling improvements from the dedicated "optimize Claude Code workflow + tools" session (a 24-agent analysis pass mapped recurring friction, then adversarially vetted each candidate for collision-safety against the parallel dev session + real ROI). All deliverables are PURELY ADDITIVE new files — no engine/data/test code changed, so no suite run required. CHANGELOG/TODO entries were deferred by that session (its handoff-writer collision contract forbids editing these two high-churn shared files); logged here now per Brian. Full detail in `docs/design/HANDOFF_tooling_additions_2026-06-12.md`.
- **2 new subagents** (`.claude/agents/`): `break-it-tester` (adversarial exploratory tester — drives the live parser via `tests/harness.py` with malformed/out-of-order/boundary input to DISCOVER breakage the 100%-confirmatory suite never asserts; read-only, report-only, high finding bar — only crash/state-corruption/error-leak/silent-swallow count) + `handoff-writer` (drafts CHANGELOG/TODO/HANDOFF text FROM the diff, every claim grounded in a real symbol; never edits CHANGELOG/TODO directly, writes at most one new HANDOFF, never commits).
- **4 new slash commands** (`.claude/commands/`, dir created this session): `/verify-drop` (fans out invariant-auditor + code-reviewer + test-runner + smoke-verifier on the diff), `/break-it`, `/log-design-call` (surgical schema'd append to design_calls_pending_brian), `/handoff`.
- **Upload-zip slimming** (`make_upload_zip.ps1` rewrite + `.gitignore`): the AI-upload zip had ballooned 5→30 MB+ (untracked `node_modules/` 25 MB + sourcebook PDF leaking past the old fixed blacklist). Script now drives its file list from `git ls-files` (+ `--others --exclude-standard`), so `.gitignore` is the single authority; added `node_modules/` to `.gitignore` + `-Lean` flag + >25 MB warning + untracked 27 stale committed `*.pyc`. Result: default 7.76 MB / lean 5.98 MB.
- **DEFERRED to Brian (touches the in-flight `.claude/settings.json`):** (a) allow-list shell parity — `git --no-pager diff/log/show` + PowerShell pytest/py_compile/git-status (PowerShell is the primary shell but has zero allow entries → every PS-routed pytest prompts); (b) deny-list `python main.py` / live-DB-rm guards (makes smoke-verifier's prose "never launch the real server" rule deterministic). Both are surgical inserts into the existing arrays — see the handoff §3 for exact lines + the `python main.py` caveat. Tracked as TOOL.settings_apply in TODO.json.
- **Rejected by the adversarial vet (recorded so they're not re-proposed):** era-cleanness PostToolUse hook, funnel-bypass hook, `/pre-commit`, count-pin reconciler, era-auditor agent — all either overlap existing guards or would block the parallel session / have >90% grep false-positive rates ("extend, don't add").

### 2026-06-13 — T5-questline arc Drop B (slices 2–5): the other 4 master trainers — *drop 35*
Completes the master-trainer content: 4 more rich questlines on the proven slice-1 template, each unlocking one t5 schematic. All 5 t5 recipes now have a trainer + a questline+rep gate; none is teachable without earning it.
- **4 questline chains** (`kind: questline`, `data/worlds/clone_wars/tutorials/chains.yaml`), each a rich 5-step arc (meet → flavor command → skill check → themed combat → certify) in a Contested/Wilds zone:
  - **"The Pit Boss's Favor"** (`master_hutt_blaster`) → `t5_top_spec_blaster_rifle`. Vossk the Armorer, Nar Shaddaa fighting pits (hutt_cartel). search → intimidation check → rival enforcer fight.
  - **"Field Test: Hyperdrive"** (`master_republic_hyperdrive`) → `t5_hyperdrive_surge_converter`. Lt. Corso Venn, Geonosis forward post (republic). scan → space-transports-repair check → CIS droid patrol.
  - **"Power Run: Ion Core"** (`master_republic_ion`) → `t5_mil_spec_ion_engine_core`. Chief Dax Orrin, Geonosis hive chamber (republic). examine → space-transports-repair check → Geonosian drone-warrior.
  - **"The Warrens Forge"** (`master_armorer_armor`) → `t5_master_grade_armor`. Tonnika the Smith, Nar Shaddaa Warrens (independent). search → armor-repair check → Warrens stalker.
- **4 trainer NPCs + 4 themed combat enemies** appended to `npcs_drop_b_t5_trainers.yaml` (all `trainer: true` / `chain_enemy_template` ai_config keys verified to survive the loader whitelist; all original CW-era characters per Q1, era-clean). The 4 remaining t5 schematics get their `trainer_npc` + `gated_by_questline`/`gated_faction`/`gated_min_rep` fields (`data/schematics.yaml`). 4 achievements cataloged (`data/achievements.yaml`).
- **Rep tuning held:** each new questline totals 17 rep (recognized; under the 22 ceiling) — completing a trial leaves the player well short of the rep-50 t5 gate, which is earned through post-questline play.
- **Verified:** `tests/test_t5_questline_content.py` extended (now 16 — a DATA-DRIVEN walkthrough that walks EVERY questline start→graduation through the production dispatcher, + an all-t5-schematics-gated invariant: 5 schematics × {real trainer, real questline, rep gate}) + reachability invariant green for all 5 chains + achievement-catalog test + 307-chain/98-crafting regression + smoke green.
- **Files:** `data/worlds/clone_wars/tutorials/chains.yaml`, `data/worlds/clone_wars/npcs_drop_b_t5_trainers.yaml`, `data/schematics.yaml`, `data/achievements.yaml`, `tests/test_t5_questline_content.py`, `docs/design/t5_trainer_questlines_design_v1.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — T5-questline arc Drop B (slice 1): "The Hermit's Trial" + lightsaber gate — *drop 34*
First vertical slice of the master-trainer content on the Drop-33 engine: one complete, walkthrough-proven questline that unlocks a t5 schematic, plus the schematic gate. The remaining 4 trainers follow this exact template in a companion drop (deliberately scoped one-at-a-time so each rich questline is proven walkable before the next — the drop 24/25 stranding-class risk).
- **"The Hermit's Trial" questline** (`master_jedi_lightsaber`, `kind: questline` in `data/worlds/clone_wars/tutorials/chains.yaml`): a RICH 5-step arc in the Jundland Wastes (contested_marches band) — meet Master Vehn Tasaal in his hidden cave → `meditate` to hear the kyber's song → `search` (skill check, diff 10) across Beggar's Canyon → defeat the Canyon Krayt-Spawn (combat) at the Krayt Dragon Graveyard → return to the Master to complete the trial. All 5 steps use REAL world-room slugs + only reachability-safe completion types (talk/command/skill_check/combat — no room_entered/item_acquired).
- **Master Vehn Tasaal** (Jedi Master, `trainer: true`, `trainer_npc` for the t5 lightsaber) + **Canyon Krayt-Spawn** (`chain_enemy_template: jundland_krayt_spawn`, the combat-step foil) in `data/worlds/clone_wars/npcs_drop_b_t5_trainers.yaml` (registered in `era.yaml`). ai_config whitelist verified to preserve `trainer`/`chain_enemy_template` (the drop-25 whitelist-drop class).
- **The t5 schematic gate** (`parser/crafting_commands.py`): `trainer_curriculum` is now async + gate-aware (new `_schematic_gate_met`); a schematic carrying `gated_by_questline` / `gated_faction` / `gated_min_rep` (added to `t5_master_crafted_lightsaber` in `data/schematics.yaml`) is filtered OUT of the trainer's curriculum until the player has GRADUATED the questline AND holds faction rep ≥ 50. The gate is ALSO enforced in `LearnCommand` (a player who knew the key could otherwise `learn` it directly — review BLOCKER) and gated schematics ALWAYS cost tuition (never the free first lesson — review MAJOR). Ungated schematics + existing trainers are unaffected.
- **Per-step reward consumer made real** (`engine/chain_rewards.py::apply_step_rewards`): per-step `credits` / `faction_rep` were authored across ALL chains but only `items` was ever delivered (a phantom-producer gap; the map-safety hook blocks deleting the reward lines from the protected chains.yaml). `apply_step_rewards` now delivers credits via the metered `chain_step_reward` faucet and rep via the `adjust_rep` funnel — closing the latent debt for every chain. **Per Brian (2026-06-13): ship for all chains + tune the rep, then "tune lower."** Since per-step rep now stacks on graduation rep, the graduation/step rep values were rebalanced TWICE on Brian's feedback. Final band: every onboarding chain lands a player at **recognized (~8–13)** in its faction — "the faction knows your name," ~10% of max — and "The Hermit's Trial" lands jedi_order = 18. NO chain reaches honored (50) or even mid-trusted; the t5 lightsaber's rep-50 gate must be earned through post-questline play (questline gate and rep floor stay SEPARATE). Pinned by `TestChainRepEconomyCeiling` (hard ceiling 22 + no-honored + questline-leaves-room-to-climb).
- **Achievement** `hermits_trial` added to `data/achievements.yaml` (the questline grants it on graduation; was a phantom producer until cataloged — caught by `test_drop_f8c2e`).
- **Verified:** `tests/test_t5_questline_content.py` (12 — real questline walks start→graduation through the production dispatcher; skill-check-fail / wrong-NPC don't advance; the schematic gate hides-then-reveals across questline+rep states; the rep-economy ceiling invariant) + `test_f8c2b4` (3 new — per-step credits faucet + rep funnel + zero-skip) + the static reachability invariant extended (`register_questline_commands` + `register_meditate_command` added to match the live server) + the chain-count pins updated to count ONBOARDING chains (9) separately from questlines + 428-test chain/questline/crafting/rewards/achievements regression green.
- **Files:** `data/worlds/clone_wars/tutorials/chains.yaml`, `data/worlds/clone_wars/npcs_drop_b_t5_trainers.yaml` (new), `data/worlds/clone_wars/era.yaml`, `data/schematics.yaml`, `data/achievements.yaml`, `parser/crafting_commands.py`, `engine/chain_rewards.py`, `tests/test_t5_questline_content.py` (new), `tests/test_f8c2b4_use_command.py`, `tests/test_chain_corpus_reachability_invariant.py`, `tests/test_f8_tutorial_chains_yaml.py`, `tests/test_f8c1_chargen_chain_selection.py`, `docs/design/t5_trainer_questlines_design_v1.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.

### 2026-06-13 — T5-questline arc Drop A: multi-slot chain engine + `mastery` surface — *drop 33*
Engine groundwork for the master-trainer questlines (`T2.DEF.t5_trainer_storyline`, design in `docs/design/t5_trainer_questlines_design_v1.md`). The chain engine was single-slot + chargen-only (`attributes.tutorial_chain`, no start-verb). This drop generalizes it to support a SECOND, deliberately-started mid-game questline slot — behavior-neutral for onboarding (all 222 legacy chain tests green). No content yet; Drop B authors the 5 questlines + trainers.
- **Multi-slot state machine** (`engine/tutorial_chains.py`): every state helper (`select_chain`, `get_current_step`, `advance_step`, `is_chain_complete`, `get_active_chain_id`, `reset_chain_state`, `get_satisfied_prereqs`, `record_prereq_satisfied`, `clear_step_progress`, `record_combat_kills`, `get_combat_kills`) gains an optional `state_key` param defaulting to `_TUTORIAL_CHAIN_KEY` — so legacy onboarding callers are byte-for-byte unchanged. New `_QUESTLINE_KEY = "active_questline"` slot + `CHAIN_STATE_KEYS` tuple. New `has_completed_chain(attrs, chain_id)` durable graduated-marker check (used by the Drop-B curriculum gate).
- **`kind` chain field** (default `"tutorial"`; `"questline"` validated against `ALLOWED_CHAIN_KINDS`): additive — an absent `kind:` loads as `tutorial`, so no existing chain YAML changes. The chargen picker (`engine/creation_wizard.py`) now skips `kind: questline` chains entirely (questlines are mid-game, never chargen-assigned).
- **Slot-aware dispatcher** (`engine/chain_events.py`): `_get_active_step`, `_try_advance`, and `get_active_step_info` take a `state_key`; the 11 public hooks now route through a new `_try_advance_all_slots` that walks every slot (onboarding + questline) so one runtime event advances whichever slot owns the matching step. The multi-enemy combat-tally pre-check accumulates per-slot. Failure-tolerant per slot.
- **Slot-aware teleport/pending-flag** (`engine/chain_graduation.py`): `apply_graduation`, `apply_step_teleport`, and `_clear_pending` take a `state_key`; `execute_pending_teleport` walks BOTH slots for a pending flag. Without this a questline's inter-step/graduation teleport would have stamped `pending_step_room_id` onto a veteran's *graduated onboarding* block — slot corruption. (Caught by the code-reviewer fan-out; fixed + regression-pinned in `TestTeleportSlotIsolation` before commit.)
- **Mid-game questline surface** (`engine/chain_events.py` + `parser/questline_commands.py`): `list_questlines` / `get_questline_offer` / `start_questline` / `abandon_questline` / `has_active_questline` back the new **`mastery`** command (`mastery [start <id> | status | abandon]`). Verb is `mastery` (NOT `quest`/`quests`/`train` — those keys are owned by the Director-AI personal-quest system, the spacer quest, and the CP-spend/tutorial `train`; collision-checked). One questline at a time; eligibility reuses `is_chain_locked_for_character` (the rep/faction gate). NPC-offer surface rides the existing `talk_to_npc` hook — talking to a questline's start-NPC while eligible surfaces "Type `mastery start <id>`".
- **Verified:** `tests/test_t5_questline_engine.py` (24 — slot parameterization + isolation, per-slot combat tally, the `kind` field + invalid-kind rejection, the full start/abandon/offer/one-at-a-time surface, the dispatcher's both-slot walk, onboarding-only-char neutrality, teleport/graduation slot-isolation regression, offer-prefers-unlocked) + the 298-test chain regression (test_f8c2b*, test_f8_*, test_f7j_*, chargen chain-selection, chain_graduation, reachability invariant) all green + smoke suite (227 passed).
- **Files:** `engine/tutorial_chains.py`, `engine/chain_events.py`, `engine/chain_graduation.py`, `engine/creation_wizard.py`, `parser/npc_commands.py`, `parser/questline_commands.py` (new), `server/game_server.py`, `docs/design/t5_trainer_questlines_design_v1.md` (new), `tests/test_t5_questline_engine.py` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.json`.
